-->

## What does it do?

Set `commit.gpgsign=false` flag in commit command in `tests/scripts/run-e2e-tests.js`

## Why is it needed?

This change allows people using GPG commit signature to run e2e tests without caring about the feature being enabled or not.

## How to test it?

### With no GPG keys

_— Baseline usage and CI_

Run e2e tests

```bash
yarn test:e2e
```

MUST work as on previous versions.

### With GPGP keys

1. Prerequisites 
 
The current git config must contain `gpgsign: true` config and have in-use GPG signing keys.

```json
{
  "commit.gpgsign": "true"
}
```

2. Run e2e tests

```bash
yarn test:e2e
```

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

ø

<!--

### What does it do?
* Allow swappability of items in the Edit view's configure the view drag&drop
* Add better visual cues to show where dragged item can be placed

**BEFORE**
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ac3eb02f-bdae-436f-aa51-81af08aeacf1

**AFTER**
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1ed2d4ff-0217-4b22-a6c0-c340cd762493

### Why is it needed?
The drag & drop of the Configure the view is limited by the size of the fields inside, but there were no visual cues to let user know that. Now the user can : 
1. re-order the fields more easily depending on the size of the fields and the remaining space in the container, 
2. create a new row with a dragged field if there's no room available in a container,
3. see where the dragged item can be dropped.
=> thus improving the UX.

### How to test it?
* Go to any content type in the CM. 
* Click an entry.
* Click on "Configure the view" in the menu on the upper right corner.
* Change the layout of the fields => **the drag & drop should be smoother.**
(* Check that you can still delete and add a field)
* Save the layout.
* Refresh page and see layout was saved correctly.

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)
https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/23161

title: fix(content-type-builder): disable display of invisible content types
body:
<!--
Hello 👋 Thank you for submitting a pull request.

To help us merge your PR, make sure to follow the instructions below:

- Create or update the tests
- Create or update the documentation at https://github.com/strapi/documentation
- Refer to the issue you are closing in the PR description: Fix #issue
- Specify if the PR is ready to be merged or work in progress (by opening a draft PR)

Please ensure you read the Contributing Guide: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

### What does it do?

Adds visibility enforcement for content types in the content-type builder to prevent direct URL access to invisible content types.

**Backend changes:**
- Added visibility check in `getContentType` controller that returns 404 when accessing an invisible content type via the API endpoint (`GET /content-type-builder/content-types/:uid`)

**Frontend changes:**
- Added visibility check in `ListView` component that redirects users away from invisible content types when accessing them directly via URL in the admin panel

### Why is it needed?

Content types marked as invisible in the content-type builder were still accessible by directly navigating to their configuration page URL (e.g., `/admin/plugins/content-type-builder/content-types/admin::transfer-token`). This bypassed the visibility setting, allowing users to view and potentially modify content types that should be hidden from the UI.

### How to test it?

1. Start a Strapi instance with the content-type builder enabled
2. Verify that `admin::transfer-token` (or any other content type marked as invisible) is not visible in the content-type builder navigation menu
3. Attempt to visit the content type directly via URL:
   - Navigate to: `http://localhost:1337/admin/plugins/content-type-builder/content-types/admin::transfer-token`
   - **Expected behavior**: The page should redirect to `/admin/plugins/content-type-builder/content-types/create-content-type` instead of displaying the transfer-token configuration page
4. Verify the API endpoint also enforces visibility:
   - Make a request to: `GET http://localhost:1337/content-type-builder/content-types/admin::transfer-token`
   - **Expected behavior**: Should return 404 status code
5. Verify visible content types still work normally when accessed directly

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

N/A

title: fix: change several file field types from string to text
body:
### What does it do?

Changes several of the text based fields in the file content-type from the upload package from string to text

### Why is it needed?

string based fields in most databases limit the total characters of the value to 255, text (which converts to the `longtext` in some databases) have practically no limit. This is useful especially for URLs where they can easily exceed this limit.

### How to test it?

Double check the database to ensure column type changes without data loss and that you can have file type URLs, file names, ect that exceed 255 characters

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Also fixes another issue but I couldn't find it, cc @christophecvb

fix(cli): transfer command ignores CLI flags and prompts unnecessarily (#24879)

* fix(cli): transfer command ignores CLI flags and prompts unnecessarily

Fixes two critical bugs in the transfer CLI command:

1. Interactive prompts hook runs unconditionally even when --from/--to
   and --from-token/--to-token flags are provided, breaking non-interactive
   usage in CI/CD pipelines and scripts. The --force flag was also ignored.

   Solution: Wrap interactive prompts in ifOptions() to only run when
   neither --from nor --to is provided.

2. determineDirection() returns URL object instead of string 'from'/'to',
   causing downstream code to fail when accessing opts[direction].

   Solution: Return string 'from' or 'to' instead of the URL object.

Closes #23688

title: chore: ctb session identifier implementation
body:
<!--
Hello 👋 Thank you for submitting a pull request.

To help us merge your PR, make sure to follow the instructions below:

- Create or update the tests
- Create or update the documentation at https://github.com/strapi/documentation
- Refer to the issue you are closing in the PR description: Fix #issue
- Specify if the PR is ready to be merged or work in progress (by opening a draft PR)

Please ensure you read the Contributing Guide: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

### What does it do?

Implemented a session identifier system for Content Type Builder (CTB) tracking events in Strapi. 

### Why is it needed?

All CTB-related tracking events now include a unique session ID that groups events within the same CTB session.

### How to test it?

With a lot of patience:
- run the tracker locally
- change the env variable STRAPI_ANALYTICS_URL to point the local tracker (http://localhost:4000)
- temporarily edit the tracker to log the events
- navigate through the CTB (AI CTB included)

all the events should have an event property `ctbSessionId` generated/updated when CTB is accessed (on mount), the server restarts after saving schema, or the user navigates away and returns to CTB

Also new `willUpdateCTBSchema` event has been implemented that fires when the save button is clicked (before save operation) and that includes all tracking properties + ctbSessionId

title: feat(core): adding clear button to boolean toggle
body:
<!--
Hello 👋 Thank you for submitting a pull request.

To help us merge your PR, make sure to follow the instructions below:

- Create or update the tests
- Create or update the documentation at https://github.com/strapi/documentation
- Refer to the issue you are closing in the PR description: Fix #issue
- Specify if the PR is ready to be merged or work in progress (by opening a draft PR)

Please ensure you read the Contributing Guide: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

### What does it do?

This pull request restores the ability to clear Boolean fields by re-introducing a “Clear” option for nullable Boolean attributes. It ensures that users can unset a previously selected true or false value and revert the field back to null.

### Why is it needed?

In version 4, Boolean fields were nullable and included a “Clear” button allowing users to reset the value to null.
In version 5, this option is missing, making it impossible to unset a Boolean once it has been set.

### How to test it?

1. Create or open an entity that includes a Boolean field.
2. Create an entry and set the Boolean field to either true or false.
3. Save the entry.
4. Reopen the entry and verify that a “Clear” button is available above or beside the Boolean field.
5. Click “Clear” and confirm:
    - The value resets to null.
    - The admin UI displays the field as unset.
    - Saving the entry persists the null value.

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/22733

title: feat(users-permissions): add refresh and logout actions to default permissions
body:
### What does it do?

  Adds missing default permissions for the `auth.refresh` and `auth.logout` endpoints to the `DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS` array in the users-permissions plugin:
  - `auth.refresh` → `public` role (allows unauthenticated token refresh)
  - `auth.logout` → `authenticated` role (requires valid access token)

  ### Why is it needed?

 403 Forbidden when calling `/api/auth/refresh`, as it was never added to `DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS`. Even with a valid refresh token, because the public role had no permission to access the endpoint.


  ### How to test it?

  **Prerequisites:**
  - Configure users and permissions with `jwtManagement: 'refresh'`

  **Test refresh endpoint (public access):**
  ```bash
  # Login to get refresh token
  curl -X POST http://localhost:1337/api/auth/local \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"identifier":"user@example.com","password":"password"}'

  # Refresh WITHOUT Authorization header (should return 200, not 403)
  curl -X POST http://localhost:1337/api/auth/refresh \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"refreshToken":"<token-from-login>"}'

  Expected: Returns HTTP 200 with new JWT and refreshToken

  Test logout endpoint (authenticated access):
  # Should require Authorization header
  curl -X POST http://localhost:1337/api/auth/logout \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer <jwt-token>"

  Expected: Returns HTTP 200 and invalidates session
```

 ### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

  Fixes #24867
  Fixes #24871
author: jhoward1994

reviews.body: This looks like the right solution, but I haven't tested it!

commits.message: feat(users-permissions): add refresh and logout actions to default permissions

title: fix(content-type-builder): ai chat message width and submit behavior
body: ## Fix message width when long content (with no breakable characters) ### What does it do? Adds `overflow-wrap: anywhere` and `word-break: break-word` CSS properties to both assistant messages and user messages to ensure long strings wrap within the chat message containers instead of overflowing. ### Why is it needed? Very long strings (e.g., URLs, concatenated text without spaces) were overflowing the message bubble containers, breaking the chat UI layout and making content unreadable. ### How to test it? 1. Open the Content-Type Builder AI chat 2. Send a message containing a very long string without spaces (e.g., "ItwouldbegreatifIcouldgrouparticlesintoseriesorcollectionslikeamulti") 3. Or trigger an AI response that contains such a long string 4. Verify that the text wraps within the message bubble boundaries instead of overflowing horizontally ## Fix submit message button ### What does it do? Adds `type="submit"` attribute to the send message `IconButton` in the Content-Type Builder AI chat input component. This enables the button to properly trigger form submission when clicked, allowing users to submit messages via the button in addition to the Enter key. ### Why is it needed? Previously, clicking the submit button in the AI chat did nothing - only pressing Enter would submit messages. This was because the `IconButton` component inside the form lacked the `type="submit"` attribute, so it didn't trigger the form's `onSubmit` handler. The Enter key worked because it was handled manually via a `onKeyDown` event in the `ResizableTextArea` component. ### How to test it? 1. Navigate to the Content-Type Builder in the Strapi admin panel 2. Open the AI chat (click the sparkle icon in the bottom-right corner) 3. Type a message in the chat input 4. Click the submit button (arrow up icon) - the message should be sent 5. Verify that pressing Enter still works as before 6. Verify that the button is disabled when the input is empty and there are no attachments ## Related issue(s)/PR(s) —

title: fix: enter key trigger in edit view form
body:
### What does it do?

It fixes the action when hitting the Enter key when the focus is inside a field of the edit page view in the content manager.
Pressing Enter in a field (except blocks fields) will perform a Save (default behaviour from a form).
Pressing Ctrl + Enter will perform a Publish (for Draft and publish entries).

### Why is it needed?

It was having a weird behaviour. Hitting the enter key would trigger the first button action of the form (when internationalization enabled, it was opening the "Fill in from another locale" modal for example).

### How to test it?

- Go to the content manager and select a content type with Internationalization enabled
- Add a new entry
- Publish
- Switch to another locale
- Fill a field and hit enter key
-> The "Fill in from another locale" shouldn't be triggered and the form should be submitted (Save action).

To test the Ctrl + Enter:
- Go to the content manager and select any content type with Draft and publish enabled
- Add a new entry
- Fill a field and hit enter key
- Press Cmd / Ctrl + Enter
-> The entry should be published.

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Resolves https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/21377

🚀

title: chore: replace deprecated Buffer.slice() with subarray() in encryption utils
body:
### What does it do?

Replaces the deprecated `Buffer.slice()` method with `Buffer.subarray()` in the data-transfer encryption utilities. The changes affect both encryption and decryption cipher creation functions in:
- encrypt.ts
- decrypt.ts

### Why is it needed?

The `Buffer.slice()` method has been deprecated in Node.js in favor of `Buffer.subarray()`. While both methods currently work the same way, using the deprecated method:
- Triggers deprecation warnings in newer Node.js versions
- Goes against Node.js best practices and recommendations
- May be removed in future Node.js releases

Migrating to `subarray()` ensures the codebase remains compatible with current and future Node.js versions and eliminates deprecation warnings.

### How to test it?

- Run the existing unit tests for the data-transfer package:
   ```bash
   cd packages/core/data-transfer
   yarn test
   ```
- Test encryption/decryption functionality by running an import and export, attempt to import an exported file from a previous version
author: Bassel17

title: fix(cli): transfer command ignores CLI flags and prompts unnecessarily
body:
### What does it do?

This PR fixes two critical bugs in the `strapi transfer` CLI command that were introduced in PR #23421:

**Bug #1: Interactive prompts always run (Issue #23688)**  
The interactive prompt hook was not properly gated with a conditional check, causing it to execute even when `--from`, `--from-token`, and `--force` flags were provided via the command line.

*Technical Changes:*
- Wrapped the interactive prompts hook (lines 54-190 in `command.ts`) with `ifOptions()` to only execute when neither `--from` nor `--to` is provided
- This prevents the prompts from running when users provide all required options via CLI flags
- Made all `ifOptions` condition callbacks async to match TypeScript signature requirements

**Bug #2: Wrong return type in determineDirection()**  
The `determineDirection()` function was returning the URL object instead of the string `'from'` or `'to'`, causing downstream code to fail when trying to use the direction as an object key.

*Technical Change:*
- Changed `return opts.from` to `return 'from'` (line 105)
- Changed `return opts.to` to `return 'to'` (line 108)
- This ensures the direction is used correctly as a string key for accessing `opts[direction]` and `opts[${direction}Token]`

### Why is it needed?

Users running the transfer command in non-interactive environments (CI/CD pipelines, automated scripts) were unable to use the command because it would always prompt for input, even when all required flags were provided:

```bash
yarn strapi transfer \
  --from https://origin.com/admin \
  --from-token abc123 \
  --force \
  --exclude files
# ❌ Still prompted for URL and token
```

This broke:
- **CI/CD pipelines**: Automated deployments would hang waiting for input
- **Scripts**: Shell scripts using the transfer command would fail  
- **--force flag**: The force flag was completely ignored
- **Non-interactive usage**: Any automated or scripted usage was impossible

### How to test it?

**Test Scenario 1: Non-interactive usage with all flags (reproduces #23688)**
```bash
# Should proceed WITHOUT prompts
yarn strapi transfer \
  --from https://source.strapi.io/admin \
  --from-token YOUR_TOKEN \
  --force \
  --exclude files

# Expected: Transfer proceeds directly
# Before fix: Would prompt for URL and token
```

**Test Scenario 2: Non-interactive push with all flags**
```bash
# Should proceed WITHOUT prompts
yarn strapi transfer \
  --to https://destination.strapi.io/admin \
  --to-token YOUR_TOKEN \
  --force

# Expected: Transfer proceeds directly
# Before fix: Would prompt for URL and token
```

**Test Scenario 3: Interactive usage without flags (should still work)**
```bash
# Should show interactive prompts
yarn strapi transfer

# Expected: Prompts for direction, URL, and token
# Should still work after fix
```

**Test Scenario 4: Partial flags (should prompt for missing)**
```bash
# Should prompt only for token
yarn strapi transfer --from https://source.strapi.io/admin

# Expected: Prompts only for missing token
# Should work after fix
```

**Unit Tests:**
```bash
yarn test:unit packages/core/strapi/src/cli/commands/transfer/__tests__/transfer.test.ts
```

New tests added:
- `--from` with auth not provided (should error)
- `--from` with source URL and token (should succeed)
- `--from` uses local Strapi destination when `--to` not specified

**Build and Type Checking:**
```bash
yarn build --projects=@strapi/strapi
yarn build:types --projects=@strapi/strapi
```

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Fixes #23688  
Caused by #23421  
Additional bug identified by @WDeenik in https://github.com/strapi/strapi/pull/23421#discussion_r2207601028
author: jamesvillarrubia

reviews.body: Looks great now, thanks for the fix

commits.message:
fix(cli): transfer command ignores CLI flags and prompts unnecessarily

Fixes two critical bugs in the transfer CLI command:

1. Interactive prompts hook runs unconditionally even when --from/--to
   and --from-token/--to-token flags are provided, breaking non-interactive
   usage in CI/CD pipelines and scripts. The --force flag was also ignored.

   Solution: Wrap interactive prompts in ifOptions() to only run when
   neither --from nor --to is provided.

2. determineDirection() returns URL object instead of string 'from'/'to',
   causing downstream code to fail when accessing opts[direction].

   Solution: Return string 'from' or 'to' instead of the URL object.

Closes #23688
fix(cli): make ifOptions callbacks async to match TypeScript signature

* fix: unable to clear and save time only fields in the content manager #24667

# Fix: Time input sends previous value to backend when cleared

## What does it do?

This fix corrects the Time input component to properly send `null` to the backend when the field is cleared, instead of sending `undefined` which gets omitted during serialization.

**Technical changes:**

1. Changed the field type to accept `null`:

```typescript
// Before
const field = useField<string>(name);

// After
const field = useField<string | null>(name);
```

2. Changed the `onClear` handler to set `null` instead of `undefined`:

```typescript
// Before
onClear={() => field.onChange(name, undefined)}

// After
onClear={() => field.onChange(name, null)}
```

**Location:** `packages/core/admin/admin/src/components/FormInputs/Time.tsx` (lines 14 and 28)

## Why is it needed?

When users cleared a time field using the clear button, the field would visually appear empty in the UI, but **the frontend was still sending the previous value to the backend**, preventing the field from being properly cleared in the database.

**Root cause (frontend bug):**

- The `onClear` handler was setting the field value to `undefined`
- The field remained with its previous value in the form state
- When saving, the frontend sent the **old time value** instead of a cleared field: `{ title: "Example", timeField: "14:30:00.000" }`
- **Result:** The UI showed an empty field visually, but the form state still had the old value, which was sent to the backend

**After fix (frontend):**

- The `onClear` handler now sets the field value to `null`
- `null` is a valid value that properly updates the form state
- The frontend sends `{ title: "Example", timeField: null }`
- The backend correctly receives `null` and clears the field in the database
- **Result:** Both UI and database are in sync - the field is properly cleared

## How to test it?

### Setup

1. Create a content-type with a `time` field
2. Create an entry and set a time value (e.g., `14:30`)
3. Save the entry

title: fix(graphql): scope status filter inheritance to query tree
body:
### What does it do?

This PR fixes an issue where custom GraphQL resolvers were incorrectly inheriting status filters from built-in queries, causing relation fields to return null when draft/publish filtering was applied.

### Why is it needed?

The rootQueryArgs introduced in PR #23737 to fix duplicate relations in draft/publish queries was request-scoped instead of query-scoped. This caused unintended cross-contamination between different root queries in the same GraphQL request.

### How to test it?

follow the example in this issue #23936
relations should not return null anymore

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

fixes #23936

title: feat(media-library): add aria-selected and click handlers for better …
body:
feat(media-library): Enhance asset selection with accessibility improvements

### What does it do?
- Adds aria-selected attribute to asset components for better accessibility
- Implements onClickAsset and onDoubleClickAsset methods to handle selection interactions
- Improves selection visibility in fields with large amounts of assets
- Provides styling options for customizing the media field appearance

### Why is it needed?
Media fields with large numbers of assets were difficult to navigate for users relying on assistive technologies. The aria-selected attribute enhances screen reader support by clearly communicating selection state. The improved selection handling and styling options allow developers to create more accessible and visually distinguishable interfaces when dealing with numerous assets in a single field.

### How to test it?
1. Create a media field with multiple assets (10+)
2. Navigate through assets using keyboard and verify aria-selected="true" is properly applied
3. Test with a screen reader to confirm selection announcements
4. Customize the styling using the new options and verify visual indicators for selection

title: CI: stale issue maintenance script
body:
This PR introduces automated workflows to help manage the issue backlog by identifying and closing stale issues, keeping our issue tracker focused and manageable.

### What does it do?

- Marks issues as stale after 1 year of no activity
- Closes stale issues after 14 days if no response
- Protected issues (exempt from closing):
	- Issues with labels: issue: security, severity: critical, severity: high, status: confirmed
	- Issues assigned to milestones
- Does not apply to pull requests

### Why is it needed?

- Reduces backlog clutter: Automatically handles inactive issues that are unlikely to be addressed
- Protects important issues: Critical, security, and confirmed issues are never auto-closed
- Clear communication: Users receive friendly notifications with 14-day grace periods
- Focused workflow: Separate workflows for general monitoring vs. targeted cleanup campaigns

title: fix(content-manager): preserve fields with visible:false during form submission
body:
### What does it do?

This PR fixes the `handleInvisibleAttributes` function to properly distinguish between:
1. **Static hidden fields** (`visible: false`) 
2. **Conditionally hidden fields** (`conditions.visible`)

**Technical changes:**
- Added early return for fields with `visible: false` to pass them through unchanged
- These fields are preserved from either `data` or `initialValues` without processing
- The function continues to properly handle `conditions.visible` (JSON Logic rules)
- Added comprehensive tests covering both scenarios

### Why is it needed?

**Problem:** 
Fields with `visible: false` were being incorrectly set to `null` when not present in form data,

**Use case:**
Plugins (like strapi-plugin-sitemap) add hidden fields that are permanently invisible
```javascript
_.set(attributes, 'sitemap_exclude', {
  visible: false,  // Permanently hidden
  type: 'boolean',
});
```
### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

fixes issue mentioned in this PR #24628

title: fix: #24121-fixed the teleportation issue when clicking checkbox
body:
<!--
Hello 👋 Thank you for submitting a pull request.

To help us merge your PR, make sure to follow the instructions below:

- Create or update the tests
- Create or update the documentation at https://github.com/strapi/documentation
- Refer to the issue you are closing in the PR description: Fix #issue
- Specify if the PR is ready to be merged or work in progress (by opening a draft PR)

Please ensure you read the Contributing Guide: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

### What does it do?

Prevents the table row’s onClick handler from firing when clicking on the checkbox by calling preventDefault() on the checkbox’s pointerdown event. This stops the browser from generating a synthetic click event that would otherwise trigger row selection logic.

### Why is it needed?

Currently, clicking a checkbox inside a table row unintentionally triggers the row’s click handler, which can cause the selection state to toggle incorrectly or override the checkbox’s intended behavior. This happens because the browser synthesizes a click event from pointerdown/pointerup, which bubbles to the row. By preventing the default on pointerdown, we block the synthetic click at the source.

### How to test it?

- Open the table listing view in media selection
- Ensure you have selectable rows with checkboxes in the first column.
- Click directly on a checkbox: 

Path: TableRows component → checkboxes in the first <Td> cell


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/73de6904-02dc-4d80-aba7-ce130753a4d0


### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Fixes [#24121 ](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/24121)
author: chaitya-titan

commits.message: fix: prevent list from jumping to top when selecting checkbox

title: CI: action to identify duplicate issues

# What does it do?
This PR adds a new GitHub Actions workflow that automatically detects and flags duplicate issues using Claude AI. The workflow:
- Can be triggered manually with an issue ID as input
- Uses the anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 to analyze issues

title: fix: add error notification when AI metadata save fails
body:
## Summary

This PR fixes a UX issue where errors during AI metadata saves fail silently in the Media Library.

Closes #24610

## The Problem

When users generate AI captions/alt text and try to save their edits, if something goes wrong (network error, API failure, etc.), the error only appears in the browser console. There's no visible notification to let the user know their changes didn't save.

This is frustrating because users might think everything worked fine, close the modal, and later wonder why their changes disappeared.

## The Solution

I've updated the error handling to use `toggleNotification` (the same pattern used throughout the admin panel) instead of `console.error()`. Now users will see a clear red error notification at the top of the page when something goes wrong.

This is very similar to the fix I made in #24596 for the Upload Settings page - same issue, same solution, just a different part of the upload plugin.

## Changes Made

- Imported `useNotification` hook in the AI Upload Modal component
- Replaced the silent `console.error()` with a proper `toggleNotification` call
- Added TypeScript type guard to safely handle error messages

## Testing

✅ Code formatting passes (Prettier)  
✅ No ESLint errors from my changes  
✅ Follows the same error handling pattern used elsewhere in the codebase  
✅ Modal stays open on error (existing behavior preserved)

The two ESLint warnings visible are pre-existing (lines 83, 87) and unrelated to this change.
author: Ayushd785

reviews.body: Looks good to me, tested and works nicely. Thanks @Ayushd785

title: fix(upload): file-type does not support commonjs
body:
### What does it do?

Replace static import with dynamic import for file-type package to support ESM-only modules in both CommonJS/ESM builds.

The maintainer mentions this approach here: https://gist.github.com/sindresorhus/a39789f98801d908bbc7ff3ecc99d99c

### Why is it needed?

file-type is ESM-only and lacks CommonJS exports. When Strapi builds its CommonJS output, the bundler fails to transform the static import to require(). This breaks app startup on Node.js < 22, which has stricter ESM/CommonJS interop. Dynamic imports work at runtime in both module formats.

### How to test it?

You will need to be able to switch node versions with something like https://github.com/nvm-sh/nvm

Switch to node 20 (`nvm install 20` if it's not already installed. `nvm use 20` to use it, `node -v` to confirm)
Create a strapi app with `npx create-strapi-app@0.0.0-experimental.f9ca9ac9518346cac58a4a3a576bc9d9d795570d test-fix --quickstart`
Update the config in `config/plugins.js`:

```
upload: {
    config: {
      security: {
        deniedTypes: ['video/*'],
      }
    }
  }
```
Run `npm run develop` there should NOT be an error
Go to the media library and try to upload a video, it should be rejected
Try to upload other file types not in the deniedTypes list, it should work


Test the same for all node versions up to 24

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Resolves https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/24829
author: markkaylor

reviews.body: LGTM, well done ✅
Tested and it works now, good job 👏

commits.message: fix(upload): file-type does not support commonjs

title: fix: unable to clear and save time only fields in the content manager…
body:
# Fix: Time input sends previous value to backend when cleared

## What does it do?

This fix corrects the Time input component to properly send `null` to the backend when the field is cleared.

**Technical changes:**

1. Changed the field type to accept `null`:

```typescript
// Before
const field = useField<string>(name);

// After
const field = useField<string | null>(name);
```

2. Changed the `onClear` handler to set `null` instead of `undefined`:

```typescript
// Before
onClear={() => field.onChange(name, undefined)}

// After
onClear={() => field.onChange(name, null)}
```

**Location:** `packages/core/admin/admin/src/components/FormInputs/Time.tsx` (lines 14 and 28)

## Why is it needed?

When users cleared a time field using the clear button, the field would visually appear empty in the UI, but **the frontend was still sending the previous value to the backend**, preventing the field from being properly cleared in the database.

**Root cause (frontend bug):**

- The `onClear` handler was setting the field value to `undefined`
- The field remained with its previous value in the form state
- When saving, the frontend sent the **old time value** instead of a cleared field: `{ title: "Example", timeField: "14:30:00.000" }`
- **Result:** The UI showed an empty field visually, but the form state still had the old value, which was sent to the backend

**After fix (frontend):**

- The `onClear` handler now sets the field value to `null`
- `null` is a valid value that properly updates the form state
- The frontend sends `{ title: "Example", timeField: null }`
- The backend correctly receives `null` and clears the field in the database
- **Result:** Both UI and database are in sync - the field is properly cleared

## How to test it?

### Setup

1. Create a content-type with a `time` field
2. Create an entry and set a time value (e.g., `14:30`)
3. Save the entry

## Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Fix [#24667](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/24667)
author: dagadevelop

reviews.body: Tested locally, LGTM! Thank you for the contribution and thanks for adding tests!

commits.message:
fix: unable to clear and save time only fields in the content manager #24667

# Fix: Time input sends previous value to backend when cleared

## What does it do?

This fix corrects the Time input component to properly send `null` to the backend when the field is cleared, instead of sending `undefined` which gets omitted during serialization.

**Technical changes:**

1. Changed the field type to accept `null`:

```typescript
// Before
const field = useField<string>(name);

// After
const field = useField<string | null>(name);
```

2. Changed the `onClear` handler to set `null` instead of `undefined`:

```typescript
// Before
onClear={() => field.onChange(name, undefined)}

// After
onClear={() => field.onChange(name, null)}
```

**Location:** `packages/core/admin/admin/src/components/FormInputs/Time.tsx` (lines 14 and 28)

## Why is it needed?

When users cleared a time field using the clear button, the field would visually appear empty in the UI, but **the frontend was still sending the previous value to the backend**, preventing the field from being properly cleared in the database.

**Root cause (frontend bug):**

- The `onClear` handler was setting the field value to `undefined`
- The field remained with its previous value in the form state
- When saving, the frontend sent the **old time value** instead of a cleared field: `{ title: "Example", timeField: "14:30:00.000" }`
- **Result:** The UI showed an empty field visually, but the form state still had the old value, which was sent to the backend

**After fix (frontend):**

- The `onClear` handler now sets the field value to `null`
- `null` is a valid value that properly updates the form state
- The frontend sends `{ title: "Example", timeField: null }`
- The backend correctly receives `null` and clears the field in the database
- **Result:** Both UI and database are in sync - the field is properly cleared

## How to test it?

### Setup

1. Create a content-type with a `time` field
2. Create an entry and set a time value (e.g., `14:30`)
3. Save the entry

## Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Fix [#24667](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/24667)

test: Time component

Add Time component tests

title: feat: improve package registry URL determination during Strapi upgrade
body:
### What does it do?

During the run of Strapi's upgrade script, when fetching information about the @strapi/strapi package, instead of forcing the use of the official NPM registry https://registry.npmjs.org/, the registry is chosen from:

1. NPM_REGISTRY_URL environment variable
2. If not found, detect the used package manager (npm or yarn) and get the registry setup for it
3. If it cannot find it, fallback to the default value https://registry.npmjs.org/

### Why is it needed?

If the official NPM registry is not accessible or the user does not want to use it for any reason, the user should be able to choose another registry.

For example, this is needed in organisation that block access to the offical registry and require the use of a controlled mirror registry.

### How to test it?

Build @strapi/upgrade and check unit tests
``` bash
yarn nx build @strapi/upgrade
yarn nx test:unit @strapi/upgrade
```

Create a Strapi project that doesn't use the lastest version. 
``` bash
npx create-strapi@5.0.5 my-strapi-project-to-upgarde
```

Run the upgrade script on the created Strapi project using the locally build @strapi/upgrade. Use the debug option to display information about used registry as well as the dry option to run multiple time the command and so try different ways to setup the registry.
``` bash
# Used the registry of the project's package manager
npx /path/to/strapi/packages/utils/upgrade/bin/upgrade.js latest --dry --debug
# Used the registry set in NPM_REGISTRY_URL
NPM_REGISTRY_URL=https://registry.yarnpkg.com npx /path/to/strapi/packages/utils/upgrade/bin/upgrade.js latest --dry --debug
```

The update fails but what we are looking for here is the line `[DEBUG] [2025-02-17T23:37:23.605Z] Using package manager registry: https://registry.npmjs.org/` that give us the used registry. Also the next few lines should show that it was able to fetch @strapi/strapi and get the last version to upgrade to.

If you got this message with the first command: 
```
[WARN]  [2025-02-17T23:47:13.204Z] Failed to determine registry URL from package manager
[DEBUG] [2025-02-17T23:47:13.205Z] Using default registry: https://registry.npmjs.org
```
Run a package dependency installation and rerun the upgrade script.

If you do not have the `Failed to determine registry`, to try the fallback to default registry, delete the lock file and the node_modules folder. 

For the test with the environment variable, you should have the log `[DEBUG] [2025-02-17T23:44:24.401Z] Using NPM_REGISTRY_URL: https://registry.yarnpkg.com`
author: mottet

commits.message:
feat: improve package registry URL determination during Strapi upgrade

- Add support for detecting registry URL from package manager configuration
- Prioritize environment variable, then package manager registry
- Handle trailing slashes in registry URLs
- Add debug logging for registry URL determination
- Add comprehensive tests for registry URL determination
chore(upgrade): refactor package manager registry detection

title: fix: unable to customize cors settings in graphql plugin
body:
### What does it do?

This PR reinstates the ability to customize CORS settings specifically for the graphql plugin in Strapi. Previously, configuring CORS under the apolloServer attribute inside the graphql plugin settings was effective. However, this functionality was removed in [this commit](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/commit/13a2f8b2468b7fa0ac27fd4f08ac47ad4e36ee31#diff-c689097227a8c063449c5940e4f97b13f193e20f6f2c2b38b2b60a29b7b8b601L127).

This PR restores the ability to define CORS settings directly in the graphql plugin configuration.

### Why is it needed?

Setting CORS in middlewares.js is not sufficient because @koa/cors is executed twice:

- Globally – as part of the application's middleware stack.
- Specifically for the GraphQL plugin – when processing GraphQL requests.

The second invocation, which occurs within the graphql plugin, does not inherit the CORS settings from plugin.js. This leads to unintended behavior where custom GraphQL CORS configurations are ignored.

### How to test it?

Specify two configs:
1. middlewares.js

```
{
    name: "strapi::cors",
    config: {
      maxAge: 31536000,
      origin: ["http://my-domain.tt"],
      methods: ['GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'PATCH', 'DELETE', 'HEAD', 'OPTIONS'],
    },
  },
```

2. in the plugins.js

```
{
...

graphql: {
    config: {
      endpoint: '/graphql',
      apolloServer: {
        cors: {
          origin: ["http://my-domain.tt"],
          methods: ['GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'PATCH', 'DELETE', 'HEAD', 'OPTIONS'],
        },
      },
    },
  },
...

}
```

try to perform a request from the application which is based on `my-domain-2.tt`

Expected:
Application should return a header:
```
access-control-allow-origin: http://my-domain.tt
```

Actual:
Application returns header:
```
access-control-allow-origin: *
```

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Let us know if this is related to any issue/pull request
author: dmaklygin

reviews.body: Thanks for this fix! Just one thought
Thanks for the contribution, it looks good now !

commits.message: fix: unable to customize cors settings in graphql plugin
fix(graphql): restore CORS configuration support with Strapi 4 compatibility

title: fix: modifying&publishing locale should only update that locale
body:
<!--
Hello 👋 Thank you for submitting a pull request.

To help us merge your PR, make sure to follow the instructions below:

- Create or update the tests
- Create or update the documentation at https://github.com/strapi/documentation
- Refer to the issue you are closing in the PR description: Fix #issue
- Specify if the PR is ready to be merged or work in progress (by opening a draft PR)

Please ensure you read the Contributing Guide: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

### What does it do?
* Only updates one locale if non-localized field(s) isn't(aren't) modified.
* Removes "publish" action from i18n middleware

### Why is it needed?
If a document has localization enabled but at least one non-localized field:
* modifying an entry in one locale shouldn't update all locale unless that/those non-localized field(s) were modified.
* publishing one locale shouldn't publish all locales

### How to test it?
* Create at least one more locale in the Internationalization settings page.
* In the Content Type Builder, create a Content Type with localization enabled, but with at least one field non-localized.
* Go to the Content Manager for this Content Type
* Create an entry in the default locale.
* Create an entry in the additional locale.
* Modify a localized field in the additional locale => only this entry should be updated in the db.
* Modify a non-localized field in the additional locale => both entries should be updated in the db.
* Publish the default locale's entry.
* Publish the additional locale's entry.
* Modify a localized field in the additional locale's entry.
* Publish the additional locale's entry => the default locale's entry shouldn't be updated nor published again.
* Modify a non-localized field in the additional locale's entry.
* Publish the additional locale's entry => the default locale's entry should also be updated but not published again (status should be **Modified**)


### Related issue(s)/PR(s)
https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/22924
author: mathildeleg

reviews.body:
Nice work! Tested and looks like it's working as expected now 👏 

I think we should merge but noticing one edge case when AI translation is enabled: 

Let's say both my locales are published. I update only localized fields in the default locale, if that locale contains a non-localized field then the additional locale will always be marked as modified after publishing the original locale even I didn't make changes to any non-localized fields. 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f93d2033-9613-44df-ae16-da50034e45b3

title: fix: prefill non translatable fields when creating new document locale
body:
### What does it do?

When creating a new locale of a document, the non translatable fields (where "Enable localization for this field" is disabled) are empty but they should be pre-filled because their values are shared between all locales.

BEFORE:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c7b0518d-2c1f-422d-a79f-2ca567598c39


AFTER:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5e03aa12-84fe-45f6-bf8e-9a4f22678aeb



### Why is it needed?

Leaving the fields empty is confusing and the user needs to put them again (or use the "Fill in from another language" feature). It can be a waste of time and the new value entered could overwrite all other locales if different from the original one.

### How to test it?

- Make sure that you have a content type that has Internationalization enabled + have at least one field with "Enable localization for this field" to false
- Go to the content manager and select this content type
- Create a new entry and fill the field with some data
- Save
- Select another locale in the language selector to create a new variation of the document
-> The field you have previously filled should remain with the value you entered instead of being reset

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Resolves #21850 #22080 #23600

🚀
author: Adzouz

reviews.body: Cannot be 100% confident about it because of the side effects that can be triggered by these changes.
Nice, good job!

commits.message: fix: prefill non translatable fields when creating new document locale
fix: avoid useless getDocument fetch and provide fields in availableLocales
fix: filter in non translatable fields

title: fix: get draft relations count from document with locale
body:
<!--
Hello 👋 Thank you for submitting a pull request.

To help us merge your PR, make sure to follow the instructions below:

- Create or update the tests
- Create or update the documentation at https://github.com/strapi/documentation
- Refer to the issue you are closing in the PR description: Fix #issue
- Specify if the PR is ready to be merged or work in progress (by opening a draft PR)

Please ensure you read the Contributing Guide: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

### What does it do?
Get `countDraftRelations` from current locale, not just for the document.

### Why is it needed?
If a single type had entries in locale(s) other than the default locale, there was an alert to display the error in fetching the `countDraftRelations` for that document. The goal here is to remove that alert by fetching the relations from the locale, not just the document, so that the correct count is returned for that locale's entry.

⚠️ _Seems like the `countDraftRelations` returns all relations (published are counted as draft relations too) instead of just draft relations for now: https://www.notion.so/strapi/Count-draft-relations-56901b492efb45ab90d42fe975b32bd8 but I'm not touching this here_

**BEFORE**
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2587e26f-65c6-4650-8201-a6f63771bafb

**AFTER**
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6e438739-c9dd-47ab-a732-6dcbe46cddd4

### How to test it?
* Go to the Settings for the Internationalization and add an additional locale 
* Go to the CTB and create a Single type with at least one field + localization enabled
* Go to the CM and create an entry in an additional locale
* Navigate between the "Create an entry" (in the default locale) and your existing entry, without creating an entry in the default locale => There should be no Alert.

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)
https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/23450
author: mathildeleg

reviews.body: works well 👍

commits.message: fix: get draft relations count from document with locale

title: chore(tests): add setupDatabaseReset utility
body:
### What does it do?

Add `setupDatabaseReset` utility. Accomplishes the same goal as `testInTransaction` by resetting the database after each test has run. It's slower but more reliable and is not vulnerable to test failures caused by function calls outside the test's transaction context when using testInTransaction. 

Updates the docker file to spin up test databases since it was throwing an error when starting

### Why is it needed?

testInTransaction often causes API tests to fail since some async code is intentionally fired without await and then tries to execute after the test's transaction context has closed

For example see: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/actions/runs/19104795038/job/54586327649?pr=24648

### How to test it?

The API tests should pass on this PR

Test it locally by running the docker test file `docker-compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up`

Now you can test all database dialects with `yarn test:api --db=<sqlite | postgres | mysql>`

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

I've tested (in the CI) this will resolves the transaction error causing tests to fail on:

https://github.com/strapi/strapi/pull/24648
author: markkaylor

reviews.body: LGTM (but I don't think I know enough on this subject to give my full approval 🙈)
LGTM 👍

commits.message: chore: replace transactions with db truncate
chore: update all use of testInTransaction
chore: use schema update for safe operations

title: feat(upload): upload file type restriction
body:
<!--
Hello 👋 Thank you for submitting a pull request.

To help us merge your PR, make sure to follow the instructions below:

- Create or update the tests
- Create or update the documentation at https://github.com/strapi/documentation
- Refer to the issue you are closing in the PR description: Fix #issue
- Specify if the PR is ready to be merged or work in progress (by opening a draft PR)

Please ensure you read the Contributing Guide: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

### What does it do?

This PR introduces MIME-type validation for file uploads in the Upload plugin.
It adds a centralized, configurable mechanism to restrict uploaded file types based on real MIME detection (using the file-type library).

Key technical changes:

- Added a new utility mime-validation.ts that detects the real MIME type from file bytes using file-type.
- Enforces allowedTypes and deniedType from plugin.upload.config.security.
- Returns structured validation results.
- When multiple files are uploaded, only valid files are uploaded; invalid files are silently filtered out and not uploaded.

### Why is it needed?

This feature addresses recurring unrestricted upload vulnerability reports.

With this change:

- File uploads are now validated based on their actual MIME signature, not the declared extension.
- Admins can explicitly control which file types are allowed.
- The system prevents accidental or malicious uploads (e.g., shell scripts, executables, disguised files).
- Provides a safer and more consistent experience

### How to test it?
Define your config in config/plugins.js:

```
export default () => ({
  upload: {
    config: {
      security: {
        allowedTypes: ['image/*', 'application/*'],
        deniedTypes: ['application/x-sh', 'application/x-dosexec']
      },
    },
    ...
  },
});
```

Testing steps:

- Start Strapi and open the Media Library.
- Upload a mix of files.
- Only valid files should appear in the Media Library.
- Invalid files will be silently ignored (not uploaded or stored) in the AI Media Library, while in the non-AI Media Library, errors will be displayed at the UI level for those files.

**Note:**
This implementation currently applies only to the backend.
UI feedback behavior differs between components:
- In the non-AI Media Library, file errors are displayed at the UI level for invalid uploads.
- In the AI Media Library, invalid files are silently filtered out and not uploaded.

Introducing unified UI feedback across both components will be considered in a future iteration, given the architectural differences between them. Moreover, introducing new UI logic is complex at this stage due to existing technical debt in the Upload plugin.

**Documentation:**

https://github.com/strapi/documentation/pull/2790

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

https://github.com/strapi/content-squad/issues/108
author: araksyagevorgyan

title: enhancement: enhance plugin generators
body:
<!--
Hello 👋 Thank you for submitting a pull request.

To help us merge your PR, make sure to follow the instructions below:

- Create or update the tests
- Create or update the documentation at https://github.com/strapi/documentation
- Refer to the issue you are closing in the PR description: Fix #issue
- Specify if the PR is ready to be merged or work in progress (by opening a draft PR)

Please ensure you read the Contributing Guide: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

### What does it do?

This PR enhances the `@strapi/generators` for it's use in plugins. Primary changes:

1. It will add the newly created service/controller/content-type to the index file
2. It will also create a routes file for plugins when using the `api` generator
3. Put all the generated plugin files in 'server/src/' instead of 'server/', this is in line with the plugin SDK initialization
4. Create custom hbs templates for plugins, allowing for the use of route factories
5. Correctly identify the use of Typescript in plugins

### Why is it needed?

Enhance the DX when using the generators to build plugins. Some of the generators (content-types & routes) are currently broken for plugins because they don't respect the format in which they need to be exported to be valid in a plugin.

### Tradeoffs

The `extend-plugin-index-files.ts` helper has gotten quite complex. I wonder if there is a better approach for this, or even a better location? I'm happy to take some feedback on this!

### How to test it?

1. Pull this branch
2. Navigate to the generators package: `cd packages/generators/generators`
3. Install dependencies: `yarn install`
4. Build and publish: `yarn build && npx yalc push --publish`
5. Setup a new strapi project: `npx create-strapi@latest my-strapi-project`
6. Navigate to the project: `cd my-strapi-project`
7. Install the updated generators package: `npx yalc add @strapi/generators`
8. Create a local plugin in your project: `cd src/plugins && npx @strapi/sdk-plugin init test-plugin`
9. Use the `yarn strapi generate` command and generate some code for your new `test-plugin`
10. Witness all the improvements
author: boazpoolman

reviews.body: Nice! Overall looks good, just a couple little things
Thanks, this is great. Maybe we can coordinate around the docs changes now ?

title: chore: update ai tracking events
body:
### What does it do?

- Create an EE api to fetch the ai settings. It is mostly needed so we can get the i18n config setting in the content manage in order to send it along with the tracking event. I chose to do it in EE because I saw other AI related code there but I also see some of the same code in admin (ie `getAIToken`), I'm confused about that but I think this is still the right place? Happy to move it to admin though if anyone disagrees.
- Only send the `isAIXConfigured` event property if ai is available on the instance. 
- Always send the `isAIXConfigured` event property if ai is available with the value true or false

### Why is it needed?

- For i18n the tracking is not sent from the right place causing duplicate events for publish. 
- When a license is added with AI and then the license is remove or AI is removed the last config is still sent to the tracking 

### How to test it?

Make sure you have a growth license with AI

### i18n
By default i18n should be disabled. Perform a save and publish action then check the tracking events. They should have an event property. `isAIi18nConfigured: false`.

Check the `track` network request to confirm:
`didPublishEntry`
`didEditEntry`

Enable AI for i18n and do the same test. You should now see `isAIi18nConfigured: true`.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df3f77b1-9da9-4ec9-8211-16ae2e1f7052

Now remove you license, do the same test, you should not see the property sent with the tracking event at all. If you add the license back it should have your previous setting still configured and continue sending tracking events.

### Media library
By default the media library is enabled. _All_ tracking events in the media library should be sending an event property `isAIMediaLibraryConfigured`. 

Upload an image and change an alt text and caption generated by AI. Check these tracking events for AI. They should have an event property. `isAIMediaLibraryConfigured: true`

`didEditAICaption`
`didEditAlAlternativeText`

Disable AI for Media Library. You should now see the tracking event property with `isAIMediaLibraryConfigured: false`

(Note: in the video I first check willNavigate that doesn't have the property, it is not sent by the upload plugin so that is fine)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/561bc01d-d537-4e8d-b738-d277190e68a4

Now remove you license, do the same test, you should not see the property sent with the tracking event at all. If you add the license back it should have your previous setting still configured and continue sending tracking events.


### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Let us know if this is related to any issue/pull request

title: fix(TypeScript): allow documentId as value in fields for document api
body:
### What does it do?

It allow to select the "documentId" in the strapi 5 document api inside selected "fields" without tricking the TypeScript to allow so.

### Why is it needed?

The requests for documentId work and return the documentId, but the typescript check does not allow it, so need to trick TypeScript with unclean `as any`.

### How to test it?

Have objects with references and only want to get the documentId of referenced object.
Do something like the following and verify TypeScript did not complain invalid `documentId` field.
```
const entry = await strapi.documents(contentType).findOne({
  documentId: entryId,
  fields: ['documentId'],
  populate: {
    operation: { fields: ['documentId', 'phase'], populate: { organization: { fields: ['documentId'] } } },
  },
});
```
Without the fix tricking TypeScript with `contentType as any` is required, but than loosing the IntelliSense help for possible fields and other checks / info.
author: swerder

reviews.body:
LGTM!
thanks for the contribution

commits.message: fix(TypeScript): allow documentId as value in fields for document api

title: chore: bump swc libs
body:
<!--
Hello 👋 Thank you for submitting a pull request.

To help us merge your PR, make sure to follow the instructions below:

- Create or update the tests
- Create or update the documentation at https://github.com/strapi/documentation
- Refer to the issue you are closing in the PR description: Fix #issue
- Specify if the PR is ready to be merged or work in progress (by opening a draft PR)

Please ensure you read the Contributing Guide: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

### What does it do?

Only bump the swc dependencies

### Why is it needed?

Not 100% but it should fix the binding issues we have on mac when installing strapi (related to swc). It is a known issue that has been reported in many swc versions https://github.com/swc-project/swc/issues/11129#issuecomment-3442338217

### How to test it?

- On Mac OS

title: fix: Unable to click on the last row in Media Library modal
body:
## Fix: Media library pagination blocking hover on bottom assets

### Issue

In the Media Library dialog, the pagination footer at the bottom was blocking hover interactions on assets positioned near it. Users couldn't hover over or interact with the last row of assets in the grid view.

### Root Cause

The pagination container had `position="relative"` and `zIndex={1}` (required for the PageSize dropdown to display correctly), which created a stacking context that was blocking pointer events to elements visually positioned behind it.

### Solution

Added the `style` property of the pagination `Flex` container from:

```typescript
style={{ overflow: 'hidden' }}
```

**Location:**
`packages/core/upload/admin/src/components/AssetDialog/BrowseStep/BrowseStep.tsx` (line 462)

### Testing

1. Open Media Library dialog
2. Navigate to a folder with many assets (enough to show pagination)
3. Hover over assets in the last row near the pagination footer
4. **Expected:** Hover effects work correctly on all assets

### Files Changed

- `packages/core/upload/admin/src/components/AssetDialog/BrowseStep/BrowseStep.tsx`

Fix [#24084](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/24084)

title: fix: reset and publish enum default value
body:
### What does it do?

Fixing the reset of enumeration inputs.
We're having the problem in the content manager, when we have a non required enum, it's currently not possible to reset its value and publish without having a validation error. The value should reset to null instead of an empty string.

Before:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0a4cddee-2642-4eb1-af4e-b3e69315b05c


After:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5cd2205f-55d2-484d-94fb-a34f4f46320c


### Why is it needed?

We should be able to reset non-required enums values and publish documents.

### How to test it?

- Go to the content manager and select a content type that has a non-required enumeration field (if you don't have any, please set it up before in the content type builder)
- Add a new entry
- Select a value in the enumeration combobox
- Click Publish
- Click again on the combobox and on "Choose here" to reset the value
- Click Publish
-> The document should be correctly saved without any validation error

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Resolves https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/24285 https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/22967 https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/24174

🚀
author: Adzouz

reviews.body: All good, well done 👍🏽

title: fix: rerendering and flickering issue on i18n fields
body:
### What does it do?

_I know it's related to admin + content manager package but flagging it as plugin:i18n to better track the issue._

There are a few weird behaviours currently with i18n fields.
The first is that a few fields don't rerender correctly with the correct data when switching locales right after a save and can cause wrong data to be overriden in the wrong locale (this one is pretty serious).

Video to show the problem:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a0d56d86-f88f-4847-9083-29f8a3f08eae


The second is a little flickering when clicking on Publish after editing fields in an entry (caused by a resetForm action triggered when publishing entries).

Video to show the problem:
_(focus on the text field to see the flickering issue when publushing)_

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/52b216c1-61d4-4d3a-839e-9c8bab0bcf5f



### Why is it needed?

It would prevent data from a locale to override content of another one in translated fields and prevent flickering issue displaying the old value for a second when clicking on Publish button.

### How to test it?

For the first issue, it's tricky but here are the steps:
- Go to the content manager and select a collection type where Draft and Publish and Internationalization are enabled and where there's a number field (also localized)
- Create a new entry in the first locale and put a number
- Save and publish
- Switch to another locale
- Put another value for the number and save + publish too
- Switch back to the first locale, change the number and save (put another number than the one you choose for the second locale to see the difference)
- Switch to the second locale again and check the value in the number field
-> Before the fix, the value would be wrong and the one you set on the first locale
-> After the fix, the value is the one you should see for the second locale

For the second issue (flickering):
- Go to the content manager and again select the same collection type than for the first test
- Put some content in any field (a text one for example)
- Click Save and Publish
- Edit the value of the field again and click Publish
-> Before the fix, for a second you'll see the previous value
-> After the fix, no more flickering, the value stays the latest

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Resolves #20409 #21063 #23765

🚀
author: Adzouz

reviews.body: Looks good to me 👌🏻
Well done @Adzouz

title: feat(i18n): update all translatable content
body:
<!--
Hello 👋 Thank you for submitting a pull request.

To help us merge your PR, make sure to follow the instructions below:

- Create or update the tests
- Create or update the documentation at https://github.com/strapi/documentation
- Refer to the issue you are closing in the PR description: Fix #issue
- Specify if the PR is ready to be merged or work in progress (by opening a draft PR)

Please ensure you read the Contributing Guide: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

### What does it do?

This PR introduces the way to correctly collect translatable content from the default locale.
It ensures only localized fields (excluding media and relation) are sent to the AI for translation, while media assets are copied from the default locale to maintain consistency across all locales (root level).
Main features:
**Translatable Content (translateableContent)**

- Extracts only fields that should be translated by AI, based on each content type’s i18n configuration.
- Uses traverseEntity to recursively collect fields marked with pluginOptions.i18n.localized === true.
- For localized components or dynamic zones, includes all child fields except media and relation.
- Produces a minimal object containing only fields to be sent to the AI translation service.
- Added some content types to test nested objects are translated.

### Why is it needed?

This PR updates the service to correctly handle the collection of translatableContent from the default locale document.

### How to test it?

1. Create or update a localized content type with translatable fields and media fields.
2. Ensure some fields are marked with pluginOptions.i18n.localized === true.
3. Create a default locale entry which will trigger AI translation to a derived locale.
4. Verify:
     
- Only translatable fields are sent to the AI service.
- Media fields (e.g., coverImage) are copied from the default locale if missing in the derived one.
- Non-localized fields, media and relations are excluded from the AI payload.


Example:
When I have the following document with localized fields I am able to get translatableContent correctly for that:

```
    "createdAt": "2025-10-20T15:52:07.050Z",
    "updatedAt": "2025-10-20T15:52:07.050Z",
    "publishedAt": "2025-10-20T15:52:07.050Z",
    "locale": null
  },
  "localizations": [
    {
      "id": 10,
      "documentId": "oi1ban0kejzi7gvvlh2nw1aa",
      "title": "Beispieltitel hier",
      "content": null,
      "createdAt": "2025-10-22T13:12:30.751Z",
      "updatedAt": "2025-10-22T16:02:36.907Z",
      "publishedAt": null,
      "locale": "de"
    },
    {
      "id": 9,
      "documentId": "oi1ban0kejzi7gvvlh2nw1aa",
      "title": "Bonjour",
      "content": null,
      "createdAt": "2025-10-22T13:12:30.748Z",
      "updatedAt": "2025-10-23T11:48:44.319Z",
      "publishedAt": null,
      "locale": "fr"
    }
  ]
}
```
=> 

```
**Translateable content generated: translateableContent** = {
  "title": "Sample title here !",
  "teamMembers": {
    "id": 19,
    "name": "Jane Smith",
    "role": "Developer",
    "organization": [
      {
        "id": 9,
        "name": "TestO",
        "address": "Test address",
        "testComponent": [
          {
            "id": 9,
            "testName": "Test Name",
            "testAnotherText": "Test Another Text"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "createdBy": {}
}
```

The translateableContent passed to ai service and it should do translations for all derived locales by translating content without changing its structure. Let's say if I have 'fr' derived locale the aiResult should look something like this:
```

      aiResult = {
        localizations: [
          {
            locale: 'fr',
            content: {
              title: 'Bonjour',
              teamMembers: {
                name: 'Jane Smith',
                role: 'Developpeuse',
                organization: [
                  {
                    id: 1,
                    name: 'TestO',
                    address: 'Test address',
                    testComponent: [
                      {
                        id: 1,
                        testName: 'Nom du test',
                        testAnotherText: 'Tester un autre texte',
                      },
                    ],
                  },
                ],
              },
            },
          },
        ],
      };
```
**NOTE**: Currently, ai service is not able to translate these kind of elements without changing its structure. Meaning, this pr can be tested whenever ai service returns a good shaped data.
I used mocked data to verify merging of translatableContent and mediaFields (both translatableContent and mergedData looked fine to me). But that is maximum that can be done without ai service.

title: fix(i18n): LocalePicker not showing all authorized locales
body:
### What does it do?

Updates the `useI18n` hook to merge locales from all roles that share the same permission on a collection type.
Previously, if a user had multiple roles with the same permission but different locales, only the locales from one role were returned.
This change uses `lodash/union` to combine locales from all applicable roles so the Locale Picker shows the complete set of authorized locales.

### Why is it needed?

Without this fix, the Locale Picker only shows locales from a single role when a user has multiple roles with the same permission but different locales.
This can confuse users, as they may think they don’t have access to certain locales, even though they can still navigate to them manually via the URL.

### How to test it?

1. Create two roles with the same permission on the same collection type but different locales:

- Role A → `canRead` on Articles for locale en

- Role B → `canRead` on Articles for locale fr

2. Assign both roles to the same user.

3. Login with that user and go to Content Manager → Articles.

4. Open the Locale Picker — it should display both locales (`en`, `fr`).

### Related issue

Fixes https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/24324

title: feat(upload): fixing ordering issue
body: ### What does it do? Fixes a bug causing file name mismatches during bulk uploads in the Media Library. ### Why is it needed? When performing a bulk upload in Media Library, the uploaded files are reordered during the upload process, causing a mismatch between the original file names (from the frontend) and the files stored in Strapi. For instance, uploading 10 images at once results in all files being successfully uploaded and having AI metadata generated, but their names no longer match the original local files. Here is an example of Uploaded files in Media Library: VS Files on local machine: As you can see, pic1.jpeg differs between the local folder and the Media Library. This mismatch occurs randomly — it's not always the first file that differs, but the naming can change unpredictably during bulk uploads. **Root Cause** This issue appears to be related to Koa's internal handling of multipart form data. During parsing, Koa may reorder files within the request before they reach the upload controller. As a result, the backend receives the files in a different order than the one sent from the frontend, which leads to mismatched file-name-to-metadata associations. ### How to test it? - Upload multiple files at once in the Media Library. - Verify that the uploaded files order, names, and metadata match the original local files. ### Related issue(s)/PR(s) Let us know if this is related to any issue/pull request

title: fix: show error notification when upload settings save fails
body: ## What does it do? Fixes a bug where the Upload Settings page (`Settings > Global Settings > Media Library`) does not display error notifications when the save operation fails. Previously, errors were only logged to the console using `console.error()`, leaving users unaware that their settings changes weren't saved. Now, the page properly shows a danger notification using the `toggleNotification` hook. ## Why is it needed? Fixes #24595 **The Problem:** When users try to save upload settings and the request fails (due to network issues, server errors, etc.), they receive no visual feedback. The error is silently logged to the console, and users assume their changes were saved successfully. **The Solution:** This PR updates the mutation's `onError` callback to display a user-facing error notification, making it consistent with how errors are handled throughout the admin panel and other parts of the upload plugin (e.g., `hooks/useRemoveAsset.ts`). ## How to test it? 1. Start Strapi in development mode 2. Go to `Settings > Global Settings > Media Library` 3. Open browser DevTools > Network tab 4. Set network to "Offline" 5. Toggle any setting (e.g., "Responsive friendly upload") 6. Click "Save" 7. **Expected:** A red error notification appears at the top of the page 8. **Before this fix:** No notification appeared (error only in console) You can also test with a real server error by temporarily modifying the endpoint or breaking the API. ## Screenshots https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f3dfbbf6-f844-41b1-9db7-6eb61869f338
author: Ayushd785

reviews.body: Thanks @Ayushd785 for the contribution!

title: fix: fill from other locale creates empty components
body:
### What does it do?

When creating another locale for a document and click on "Fill in from another locale", it creates empty components if they weren't provided at all in the locale to copy data from.
The correct behaviour should be to reproduce the exact same content from the source locale. If the component doesn't exist, then it shouldn't exist either after copying content.

### Why is it needed?

It's a weird issue that doesn't makes sense and could lead to validation error when submitting the form.

### How to test it?

- Go to the content manager and choose a collection type that has a component (not repeatable) and has internationalization enabled
- Create a new entry in a locale and leave the component empty
- Switch to another locale (with the locale selector of the edit view) to create a new document
- Click on "Fill in from another locale" icon
-> The component should stay empty and not having all its fields appearing

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Resolves https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/22449

🚀

body:
### What does it do?

Update the document header actions API to accept a `customizeContent` with `icon` to display a popover for a status icon. I need feedback here on the API. I tried to follow what was done for dialog, but maybe it should be more explicit with a new type 'status' instead of leveraging type `icon` with `customizeContent`. In [the first commit](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/pull/24585/commits/ca1e9608af73088ba20d10f50d8d9ac67d4707af) I just had everything in the i18n plugin 

Use the new API to display the status of the AI settings for i18n in a popover

### Why is it needed?

So the user can see if AI is enabled and easily enable/disable it

### How to test it?

Go to a content-type with i18n
You should see the status icon in the document header actions
Hover it, it should display a popover based on your ai setting state with a link to settings

When AI translation is disabled
We see the status sparkle icon with instruction to enable + fill in from locale button

When AI translation is enabled
We the status sparkle icon with instruction to disable (no fill in from locale)

Remove your license, you should only see the standard 'fill in from another locale' button

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

resolves https://github.com/strapi/content-squad/issues/97

TODO:

- add e2e tests ✅ 
- Update strapi docs
author: markkaylor

reviews.body: I like the new API!
Just a tiny comment, otherwise looks clean, good job! ✨
Teste, it works perfectly 👍
Let's goo 🚀

title: fix: number and boolean values in JSON fields break admin panel
body:
### What does it do?

This PR converts `object`, `number`, and `boolean` data types to strings using `JSON.stringify`, as the `JSONInput` component from the Strapi design system requires string inputs.

### Why is it needed?

When saving a JSON field containing a `number` or `boolean` value, the following errors occur:

1. **`(config2.doc || "").split is not a function`** – This error appears when creating a new entry.
2. **`Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')`** – This error occurs when updating an existing entry.

### How to test it?

1. Create a new content type with a single **JSON** field.
2. Create a new entry and enter values such as `42` or `true` into the JSON field.
3. Attempt to save the entry, and it should now save successfully without errors.

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Fixes #22565
author: Dhruv-Maradiya

reviews.body:
Hi @Dhruv-Maradiya, thanks a lot for the contribution and sorry for the delay 🙏
I tested and everything looks good to me 🚀

title: fix: add await to prevent redirect during test
body:
### What does it do?

We have a very very annoying flaky test recently that (too) often fails our pipelines.
After investigation and thanks to the Playwright trace, I figured out that at some point in the test, there's a redirect to the Content Manager home link. I think it's caused by the lack of await on the clickAndWait call (causing redirect to happen later). Actually since we're using navToHeader, the first clickAndWait is useless. We can just remove it.

### Why is it needed?

To remove a lot of frustration in contributors waiting for pipelines to pass and has to retrigger the failed tests (sometimes a few time).

### How to test it?

- I have to admit I have no 100% guarantee that it will fix the issue because it's hard to test but let's say I am 99% confident.

🚀
author: Adzouz

reviews.body:
That was such a silly issue 😭 

You're a legend for finding it 🚀
