title: fix(content-manager): non-unique keys for Dynamic Zone add above/below menu items
body:
### What does it do?

Fixes a `Encountered two children with the same keys` React warning in the Content editor.
This error occurs when expanding the `Add component above` / `Add component below` menu items in Dynamic Zone fields.

### Why is it needed?

This error is displayed in the developer console:

```
Warning: Encountered two children with the same key, `content.text`. Keys should be unique so that components maintain their identity across updates. Non-unique keys may cause children to be duplicated and/or omitted — the behavior is unsupported and could change in a future version.
```

### How to test it?

1. Add a Dynamic Zone field to a content type.
2. Add/edit a document with this type.
3. Add an entry to the field.
4. Open the browser console.
5. Open the `Add component above` / `Add component below` menu items.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2130e672-e788-4aac-8401-00555ccff9c1)

(The buttons themselves don't work in v5.2.0, but that is a different issue.)

title: fix: keyboard accessibility in Content-Type Builder field actions
body:
Fix Keyboard Accessibility in Content-Type Builder

## Issue Validated ✅

The reported accessibility issue exists in the Content-Type Builder. Keyboard and screen reader users cannot activate the Edit and Delete buttons for fields in the field list.

## Root Cause

1. **Event Handler Type Mismatch**: The `handleDelete` event handler was typed as `React.MouseEvent`, which doesn't accept keyboard events.
2. **Missing Keyboard Event Handlers**: The Edit and Delete `IconButton` components didn't have explicit `onKeyDown` handlers to handle Enter and Space key presses.
3. **Missing Keyboard Sensor**: The drag-and-drop functionality only used `PointerSensor`, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.

## Changes Made

### 1. AttributeRow.tsx (`packages/core/content-type-builder/admin/src/components/AttributeRow.tsx`)

#### Updated Event Handler Types
- Changed `handleDelete` to accept both `React.MouseEvent | React.KeyboardEvent`
- Changed `handleClick` to accept both `React.MouseEvent | React.KeyboardEvent` (optional parameter)

#### Added Keyboard Event Handlers
- Added `handleEditKeyDown`: Listens for Enter or Space key and triggers `handleClick`
- Added `handleDeleteKeyDown`: Listens for Enter or Space key and triggers `handleDelete`

#### Updated IconButtons
- Added `onKeyDown={handleEditKeyDown}` to Edit IconButton
- Added `onKeyDown={handleDeleteKeyDown}` to Delete IconButton

### 2. List.tsx (`packages/core/content-type-builder/admin/src/components/List.tsx`)

#### Added Keyboard Support for Drag and Drop
- Imported `KeyboardSensor` and `sortableKeyboardCoordinates` from `@dnd-kit`
- Updated `sensors` configuration to include both `PointerSensor` and `KeyboardSensor`
- Used `sortableKeyboardCoordinates` for proper sortable keyboard navigation

## Testing Performed

✅ `yarn lint` - All checks passed  
✅ `yarn setup` - Build completed successfully  
✅ Code follows existing patterns from `useKeyboardDragAndDrop` hook

## Expected User Impact

After this fix:
- ✅ Keyboard users can press Enter or Space on the Edit button to open the edit modal
- ✅ Keyboard users can press Enter or Space on the Delete button to delete a field
- ✅ Keyboard users can use arrow keys to reorder fields via drag and drop
- ✅ Screen readers will properly announce button actions
- ✅ Full WCAG 2.1 compliance for keyboard navigation

## How to test

1. Navigate to Content-Type Builder
2. Create or open a content type with at least one field
3. **Using only keyboard**:
   - Tab to the Edit button on a field
   - Press Enter or Space - the edit modal should open ✅
   - Tab to the Delete button on a field  
   - Press Enter or Space - the delete confirmation should appear ✅
   - Focus on the Drag button and use arrow keys to reorder fields ✅

## Testing with Screen Reader

1. Enable a screen reader (NVDA/JAWS on Windows, VoiceOver on Mac)
2. Navigate to Content-Type Builder field list
3. Verify buttons are announced correctly
4. Verify Enter/Space keys activate buttons

## Screenshots/Videos

title: chore(upload): track ai usage
body:
Leaving this in draft as I need to clarify with Paridhi on Monday

### What does it do?

- Sends `didEditAICaption` or `didEditAIAlternativeText` when ai metadata is edited by the user
- Sends group property with the weekly upload cron to track whether or not the feature is enabled
- Sends `didUploadImage` when an image is included in the upload payload

### Why is it needed?

To learn more about the usage of the feature

### How to test it?

Add some images in the upload modal, then edit alt text or caption, click finish. Check the network tab for the `didEditAICaption` or `didEditAIAlternativeText` events

Put a log in `packages/core/upload/server/src/bootstrap.ts`
```
const res = await getService('weeklyMetrics').computeMetrics();
console.log('bootstrap*', res);
```

You should see:
```
{
  //...
  aiMetadata: true
}
```

Put a log here `https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/5cc2aeb9727e27c6bdfe149453cd172d61016987/packages/core/upload/server/src/controllers/admin-upload.ts#L120-L122`, if the payload includes and image you should see your log

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Resolves https://github.com/strapi/content-squad/issues/102

- Play around with the customization.
- Go to another workspace in the platform (for example the Content Manager) and go back to the Homepage to confirm your changes were saved.

title: [Session Manager] support origin configs and add fluent api
body:
### What does it do?

this refactors session manager to use an origin based fluent api:

```
strapi.sessionManager.defineOrigin('admin', { lifespansGoHere });

strapi.sessionManager('admin').validateAccessToken(token);

strapi.sessionManager('invalidOrigin') // throws error
```

and adds the origin definitions to the appropriate places, where they load their own configs

### Why is it needed?

- allows user code to use session manager to define their own origins
- cleaner session manager api

title: feat: responsive navigation
body: ### What does it do? Yes, you heard it right, responsiveness on Strapi CMS is now ongoing 🚀 _([one of our most requested feedback](https://feedback.strapi.io/customization/p/responsive-administration-panel))_ Starting with: - Implementation of a responsive navigation (top navigation with quick access links + burger menu) - Side navigation of the Content Manager and Settings are now full pages on mobile (accessible from the links in the navigation) - Unifying margins across the CMS pages - Login / Register pages fully responsive - Responsive NPS survey - Disabling the Guided Tour on mobile First step of many to come to improve the UX on mobile and tablets. _For the reviewers: I know this one is heavy and contains a lot of adjustments. Take your time, you can also go by commit if you prefer. Don't hesitate to challenge 🙏_ _💡 Tip: You can skip all snapshots updates_ ⚠️ Since a lot has changed, there's also a lot of work to do in the tests so please be patient while I'm trying to fix all of them 🙏 ### Why is it needed? To improve the experience of the users on mobile + tablet devices (finally 🙌). ### How to test it? - Log to the admin - Resize the window (or use the console and the device tool) and play with the navigation elements ✨ -> You might want to test all pages at different breakpoints to check non-regression ### Related issue(s)/PR(s) Waiting for: [Design System] Improve modals and dialogs: https://github.com/strapi/design-system/pull/1957 [Design System] Release RC 30: https://github.com/strapi/design-system/pull/1953 Resolves: https://github.com/strapi/content-squad/issues/88 [CMS] Bump DS version: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/pull/24456 🚀📱

title: Fix spelling errors in error messages
body:
### What does it do?

Fixes spelling errors in error messages. Changed 'occured' to 'occurred' in three files:
- DatabaseError constructor default message
- ApplicationError constructor default message  
- API error handler unknown error message

### Why is it needed?

The word 'occured' is misspelled. The correct spelling is 'occurred' with two r's. This improves code quality and professionalism in error messages shown to developers.

### How to test it?

These are default error messages that appear when errors are thrown. To verify:
1. The changes are in error constructor default messages
2. Check the modified files to confirm the spelling is now correct
3. No functional changes, only text corrections

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

None - simple typo fix found while reviewing the codebase

title: fix: reset selection when folder changes to hide bulk actions
body:
closes #24256

### What does it do?

I added an effect to watch for the change in `query.folder`. If the directory changes, the selection of the folders in the media gallery is restted and eventually the bulk actions buttons are hidden.

### Why is it needed?

Because without it, if a user selected a folders/files then navigated to another folder, the bulk actions can be applied to the items added previously leading to potential troubles.

### How to test it?

1. Open the media manager
2. Click the checkbox on a folder or an item, the bulk actions bar will appear
3. Change the directory

The bulk actions bar will be hidden.

Check the video on the first issue [here](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/24256#issuecomment-3241890361)

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

#24256
author: ahmadalfy

reviews.body: Looks good, thank you! 🙏🚀

commits.message:
fix: reset selection when folder changes to hide bulk actions

closes #24256

title: feat(upload): generating metadata from thumbnail
body:
<!--
Hello 👋 Thank you for submitting a pull request.

To help us merge your PR, make sure to follow the instructions below:

- Create or update the tests
- Create or update the documentation at https://github.com/strapi/documentation
- Refer to the issue you are closing in the PR description: Fix #issue
- Specify if the PR is ready to be merged or work in progress (by opening a draft PR)

Please ensure you read the Contributing Guide: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

### What does it do?

This update changes the AI metadata generation source in Strapi.
Previously, the metadata was generated from the original uploaded files.
With this change, the metadata will now be generated from the thumbnail versions of the files instead.

### Why is it needed?

Generating metadata from original files can be unnecessarily heavy and resource-consuming, especially for large files.
By using thumbnails instead, we reduce processing time, improve performance, and optimize resource usage without losing meaningful information for AI metadata extraction.

### How to test it?

Go to `Media Library` -> `Add new assets` -> you should see metadata has been generated by ai service.

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Let us know if this is related to any issue/pull request

title: feat(homepage): add endpoints for widget move and resize actions
body:
<!--
Hello 👋 Thank you for submitting a pull request.

To help us merge your PR, make sure to follow the instructions below:

- Create or update the tests
- Create or update the documentation at https://github.com/strapi/documentation
- Refer to the issue you are closing in the PR description: Fix #issue
- Specify if the PR is ready to be merged or work in progress (by opening a draft PR)

Please ensure you read the Contributing Guide: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

### What does it do?

Adds backend support for persisting and retrieving the homepage layout (widget order and widths) per user.

### Why is it needed?

The admin homepage now allows dynamic widget resizing and reordering. Backend persistence ensures user preferences survive refreshes and sessions.

### How to test it?

1. Run the Strapi.
2. Log in with an authenticated admin user.
3. Call (you can use curl, Postman, or any other HTTP client):

#### GET  
```
GET /admin/homepage/layout
Authorization: Bearer <your-admin-token>

Response when layout exists:
{
  "version": 1,
  "widgets": [
    { "uid": "plugin::content-manager.last-published-entries", "width": 6 },
    { "uid": "plugin::content-manager.last-edited-entries", "width": 8 }
  ],
  "updatedAt": "2025-09-11T12:34:56.000Z"
}
Response when no layout exists yet:
null
```

#### PUT
```
PUT /admin/homepage/layout
Authorization: Bearer <your-admin-token>

Request body:
{
  "widgets": [
    { "uid": "plugin::content-manager.last-published-entries", "width": 6 },
    { "uid": "plugin::content-manager.last-edited-entries", "width": 8 }
  ]
}

Response:
{
  "version": 1,
  "widgets": [
    { "uid": "plugin::content-manager.last-published-entries", "width": 6 },
    { "uid": "plugin::content-manager.last-edited-entries", "width": 8 }
  ],
  "updatedAt": "2025-09-12T09:45:00.000Z"
}
```

Notes:
version (defaults to 1) and updatedAt (set automatically on the backend to the current datetime) are optional in the request.

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

https://github.com/strapi/content-squad/issues/77

title: fix: add handling for AI credit limit exceeded error in chat component
body:
### What does it do?

  Adds handling for a new AI credit limit exceeded error in the AI chat component. The fix:
  - Introduces a new error constant LICENSE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED_ERROR
  - Displays an error alert with a danger variant when users exceed their AI credit limit

### Why is it needed?

  The AI chat component was not properly handling the "AI credit limit exceeded" error from the
  backend

### How to test it?

  1. Set up a Strapi instance with AI features enabled
  2. Use a license with limited AI credits
  3. Exhaust the available AI credits by making multiple chat requests
  4. Verify that the error message "AI credit limit exceeded" appears as a danger alert in the chat
  interface

title: feat(ai): AI Chat for Schema Creation
body:
### Welcome to the all new 🔥 AI powered Content Type Builder 🔥 

- Rapidly generate Strapi content types and components from natural language prompts or assets (code, Figma, images).
- Create content types and components in Strapi by simply describing what you need in plain language or uploading design assets.
- Transform your content ideas into fully-structured Strapi content types using AI-powered natural language processing and asset analysis.
- Streamline content modelling in Strapi with AI that converts your descriptions or visual assets into production-ready content types and components.
- Automatically generate Strapi content structures from conversational prompts or existing design artifacts like Figma designs and code snippets.
- Skip manual content type creation with an AI assistant that builds Strapi components based on your natural language descriptions or imported assets.

### Why is it needed?

- Manual setup is slow and error-prone - the AI powered CTB speeds up content schema creation to a fraction of the manual effort
- Experiment rapidly - because the system generates a first draft of the content structure that can be reviewed and edited, there is less pressure to get it right on the first try. 
- Simple iteration - all schemas can be iterated quickly, ideas tested and adjusted, all with natural language requests
- Start with a huge existing asset base easily - upload your Figma prototype or even just an image of your existing schemas and you'll be ready to start editing and using them in minutes

### How to use it?

A Growth license or Growth trial license are required for access to all Strapi AI features. With this license, you will find our integrated chatbot available in your Content Type Builder.

body: ### What does it do? - Adds a toggle in **Settings → Media Library** to enable/disable AI image metadata generation. - Updates backend validation (`settings.ts`) and adds checks in upload service and controller. ### Why is it needed? To allow admins to control whether AI metadata generation is active. ### How to test it? 1. Go to **Settings → Media Library**. 2. Toggle **AI metadata generation** on/off. 3. Verify backend behavior: uploads and responses respect the setting. #### Example requests **GET** `http://localhost:1337/upload/settings` Response: ```json { "data": { "sizeOptimization": true, "responsiveDimensions": true, "autoOrientation": false, "aiMetadata": true } } ``` **PUT** `http://localhost:1337/upload/settings` Body: ```json { "sizeOptimization": true, "responsiveDimensions": true, "autoOrientation": false, "aiMetadata": true } ``` Response: ```json { "sizeOptimization": true, "responsiveDimensions": true, "autoOrientation": false, "aiMetadata": true } ``` ### Related issue(s)/PR(s) - [content-squad#89](https://github.com/strapi/content-squad/issues/89) - [Design](https://www.figma.com/design/CUU2h7RqzMqcf9BiWFqykm/Media-Library?node-id=9-15927&p=f&t=Ae2Q344CSTbXD4wW-0) 15927&p=f&t=Ae2Q344CSTbXD4wW-0)

title: AI media lib bulk update
body:
What does it do?

Adds new route for bulk update.

Why is it needed?

Currently, the media library only allows updating files one by one. This new route makes it possible to update the file information for multiple files at once (bulk update).

How to test it?

Run the Strapi.
Log in with an authenticated admin user.
Call (you can use curl, Postman, or any other HTTP client):

POST /upload/actions/bulk-update
Authorization: Bearer <your-admin-token>

Request body:
{
  "updates": [
    { "id": 3, "fileInfo": {"name": "img3", "alternativeText": "altText1", "caption": "capt3"}},
    {"id": 4, "fileInfo": {"name": "img4", "alternativeText": "altText2"}}
  ]
}

Response:
[
    {
        "id": 3,
        "documentId": "q2t1gpuss2h3s460gc5ufa7m",
        "name": "img3",
        "alternativeText": "altText1",
        "caption": "capt3",
        "width": null,
        "height": null,
        "formats": null,
        ...
    },
    {
        "id": 4,
        "documentId": "mon3o8rjrswcuac3kxjp7r0s",
        "name": "img4",
        "alternativeText": "altText4",
        "caption": "capt4",
        "width": 1024,
        "height": 1024,
        ...
    }
]

Related issue(s)/PR(s)

https://github.com/strapi/content-squad/issues/83

title: fix: support auth.options config in sessions
body:
### What does it do?

This PR restores support for the legacy admin.auth.options configuration while introducing the new
  admin.auth.sessions.options configuration pattern. It ensures that existing JWT configurations using
  custom algorithms (like RS512) continue to work after the recent JWT enhancements in v5.24.1.

### Why is it needed?

Removing it was an unintentional breaking change

This allows user configs to pass in options directly to the jwt methods (`sign`, `decode`, etc) as was done before the recent JWT enhancements

### How to test it?

1. Test legacy configuration: Set admin.auth.options with asymmetric algorithms (RS256, RS512, ES256)
   and verify JWT generation/validation works
2. Test new configuration: Use admin.auth.sessions.options with both symmetric and asymmetric
algorithms
3. Test precedence: When both are configured, admin.auth.sessions.options should take priority
4. Run tests: The included test suites cover symmetric/asymmetric algorithms and option
merging

Admin config from reported issue https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/24457#issuecomment-3338729639

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

fixes https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/24457

## Manual Test Plan - Testing asymmetric Algorithm (e.g. with RS512) Configuration

#### Option 1: Legacy Configuration (admin.auth.options)

Add this to your config/admin.js file:

```
const fs = require('fs');

module.exports = ({ env }) => ({
auth: {
    secret: env('ADMIN_JWT_SECRET', 'your-secret-here'),
    options: {
    algorithm: 'RS512',
    privateKey: fs.readFileSync('./private-key.pem', 'utf8'),
    publicKey: fs.readFileSync('./public-key.pem', 'utf8'),
    issuer: 'your-app-name',
    audience: 'admin',
    expiresIn: '1d'
    }
}
});
```

#### Option 2: New Configuration (admin.auth.sessions.options)

Add this to your config/admin.js file:
```
const fs = require('fs');

module.exports = ({ env }) => ({
auth: {
    secret: env('ADMIN_JWT_SECRET', 'your-secret-here'),
    sessions: {
    options: {
        algorithm: 'RS512',
        privateKey: fs.readFileSync('./private-key.pem', 'utf8'),
        publicKey: fs.readFileSync('./public-key.pem', 'utf8'),
        issuer: 'your-app-name',
        audience: 'admin'
    },
    accessTokenLifespan: 30 * 60, // 30 minutes
    maxRefreshTokenLifespan: 30 * 24 * 60 * 60, // 30 days
    }
}
});

```

#### Generate private key
openssl genrsa -out private-key.pem 2048

#### Generate public key
openssl rsa -in private-key.pem -pubout -out public-key.pem

Testing Steps

1. Before the fix: Configure RS512 as above, start Strapi, try to login to admin panel
- Expected failure: "secretOrPrivateKey must be a symmetric key when using HS256" error
2. After the fix: Same configuration should work
- Expected success: Admin login works, JWT tokens use RS512 algorithm
author: innerdvations

reviews.body: Tested for both options, admin login is working and token is using RS512, LGTM!

commits.message: fix: support auth.options config in sessions
test(token): add TypeScript error suppression for mock config in token tests

### What does it do?

add conditional field count tracker

### Why is it needed?

because

### How to test it?

1. in `packages/core/core/src/services/metrics/sender.ts` OR do a global find/replace (necessary if you are testing some events that happen outside of normal Strapi usage) 
2. replace the `https://analytics.strapi.io/`string to be `http://localhost`
You may have to replace it in several places, and when starting your Strapi server, you should see your localhost (http://localhost/) URL in the logs rather than analytics.strapi.io
3. Build Strapi again so that it builds with that URL
4. Run the command `npx http-echo-server 80` in a terminal window
NOTE: if you don't have port 80 available for this purpose, you’ll need to add a port to the previous line such as 'http://localhost:1234'
5.In another terminal window, start Strapi (or run the CLI command desired, whatever is sending the analytics you are testing) and do whatever action desired to send telemetry events

You should see those events sent by Strapi in the terminal window where the http-echo-server is running

You should see `"numberOfConditionalFields` and its value changes as you add / delete conditional field

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

DX-2087

title: fix: syncing components in draft and publish relations
body:
### What does it do?

- Introduces a new findComponentParent method.

- checks if a component belongs to a draft and publish content-type before proceeding with syncing and filters those components out.

### Why is it needed?

- check issue #23858

### How to reproduce

On develop you can do the following to reproduce the bug and see the created ghost relation

Create A content type with draft and publish that has a component containing a manyWay relation also with draft and publish

Create a draft entry including a relation in the component (also in draft)
It creates an entry in the join table for the component relation

Publish the component relation
It creates another entry (ghost/orphaned relation) in the join table

Publish the parent entry containing the component with relation
It creates another entry in the join table


### How to test the fix?

Experimental for testing: `0.0.0-experimental.0e5c9562e7bee0ab55e14e247b87a12c3b0ecb40`

**Parent and relation with draft and publish**

Use a database viewer to monitor the component <> relation join table (eg components_compo_relation_lnk)

Create A content type with draft and publish that has a component containing a manyWay relation also with draft and publish

Create a draft entry including a relation in the component (also in draft)
It creates an entry in the join table for the component relation

Publish the component relation
It  DOES NOT create another entry (ghost/orphaned relation) in the join table

Publish the parent entry containing the component with relation
It creates another entry in the join table

Check the API response to ensure the relations are returned as expected

**Parent without draft and publish, relation with draft and publish**

Create A content type WITHOUT draft and publish that has a component containing a manyWay relation WITH  a draft and publish content type

Create an entry including a relation in the component in draft
It creates an entry in the join table for the component relation

Publish the relation
It creates another entry in the join table (both entries should have the same id for <compo_name>_id)

see issue #23858

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Partial fix (but incomplete) for #23858

Repair tool for existing ghost relations will come in a follow-up
author: Bassel17

reviews.body:
LGTM! Tested and fixes the issue for newly created relations.

I'm unable to reproduce the bug from the video posted by @laurenskling but even assuming there are some cases where that is happening, it appears that it's the same bug, just that it may not be 100% solved rather than introducing a new issue.

So we will go with this fix and release it Wednesday, but will not consider the issue closed until after the follow-up repair script and a bit of real-world testing to confirm whether or not there are still some edge cases where the bug remains.

title: feat: guided tour for ai ctb
body: ### What does it do? For the new AI chatbot in the Content Type Builder, that helps the user creating schemas based on their prompt, we needed to add specific steps for the new guided tour. ### Why is it needed? The goal is to show and explain to the user the role of the chatbot when AI features are enabled on Strapi. ### How to test it? - Enable AI features on your Strapi project (need to have a valid Enterprise Edition licence) - Set the `STRAPI_ADMIN_AI_URL` is the .env (you'll need the API to be running) - Be sure that the Guided Tour is reset (you can do it in the Profile settings) - Go to the Content Type Builder -> You should have the AI chatbot button on the bottom right of the screen. If not check the configuration on how to enable the AI features again. - Click on Next on the first step (should be identical to the one when AI is disabled) - The tooltip regarding the AI chatbot should be displayed and the chatbot should be opened automatically - Enter a prompt to generate a model - If the model has also fields in it, it will automatically show the next tooltip that tells the user to Save their changes - If not, ask the prompt to create a new field in the new schema to display the tooltip - After clicking on Next and saving, the Finish tooltip should be displayed -> There are only 3 steps + Finish on this tour -> If a tour was already ongoing and the AI features are enabled/disabled after, then the tour should be reset to its first step ### Related issue(s)/PR(s) Related ticket: https://github.com/strapi/content-squad/issues/78 🚀

### What does it do?

### Why is it needed?

Describe the issue you are solving.

- It uses the previous schema stored in the `strapi_database_schema` table to compare changes to not recreate indexes as the order of the columns array is the same. It also avoids doing a lot of queries to the database to know the current schema if it was already stored in the `strapi_database_schema` table.

title: future(upload): generate image metadata on file upload
body:
<!--
Hello 👋 Thank you for submitting a pull request.

To help us merge your PR, make sure to follow the instructions below:

- Create or update the tests
- Create or update the documentation at https://github.com/strapi/documentation
- Refer to the issue you are closing in the PR description: Fix #issue
- Specify if the PR is ready to be merged or work in progress (by opening a draft PR)

Please ensure you read the Contributing Guide: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

### What does it do?

- moves the admin's getAiToken into a service (so we can retrieve the token from the upload plugin)
- create an aiMetadata service on the upload plugin with:
  - isEnabled method: check if feature should be on
  - processFiles method: takes files, sends them to the ai server, returns the metadata
- hooks the upload controller to this processFiles method.

### How to test it?

- Check that getAiToken is not broken. For this, clear session storage, refresh and open the CTB chat.
- Upload an image from the media library page. Then click on the pen icon to edit its metadata. You should see the AI-generated caption and alt text already there
- Upload multiple files at once. Some that are images, some that are not. Only the images should get AI metadata. Make sure the metadata is for the right images

title: feat: add support for custom field attributes in schema mapping
body:
<!--
Hello 👋 Thank you for submitting a pull request.

To help us merge your PR, make sure to follow the instructions below:

- Create or update the tests
- Create or update the documentation at https://github.com/strapi/documentation
- Refer to the issue you are closing in the PR description: Fix #issue
- Specify if the PR is ready to be merged or work in progress (by opening a draft PR)

Please ensure you read the Contributing Guide: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

### What does it do?

### Why is it needed?

### How to test it?
`examples/getstarted/src/index.js`
```
  register({ strapi }) {
    let routes = [];
    for (const [_, api] of Object.entries(strapi.apis)) {
      for (const [_, route] of Object.entries(api.routes)) {
        console.log('routes: ', api.routes);
        routes.push(route.routes);
      }
    }
    console.log('###################');
    console.dir(routes);
    console.log('###################');
  },
```

Without this change, running this code in the register function will cause the error 

`Error: Unsupported attribute type: customField ││   at mapAttributeToSchema`

Code like this should be run in a plugins `bootstrap` instead

Running it in register means `packages/core/core/src/core-api/routes/validation/mappers.ts` is invoked before `convertCustomFieldType` from Strapi core has run - & therefore custom field attribute types can end up being processed by the mappers

Existing plugins may already be running code like this and therefore we need a fallback for custom field processing with similar logic to  `convertCustomFieldType`

experimental: `0.0.0-experimental.75524da74c8427faada874289db9595b2831263a`

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Would replace https://github.com/strapi/strapi/pull/24304
fixes https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/24083
author: jhoward1994

reviews.body:
Code LGTM but I haven't tested it yet. Let's see if any of the reporters of the issue can ensure it solves their issue.

We will follow up with an investigation of why any `type:'customField'` is present at this level, but that's an internal refactoring we can solve later.

commits.message: feat: add support for custom field attributes in schema mapping

title: security(deps): update form-data, axios, and sha.js
body:
!!! HOTFIX FOR Release 5.23.5 !!!

### What does it do?

updates `form-data`, `axios`, and any pinned versions of those and `sha.js`

NOTE: there is still a lingering subdependency to form-data 2.3.3 remaining due to a dependency on node-ses that does not have an update, which is used by `@strapi/provider-email-amazon-ses`

```bash
npm why form-data@2.3.3
form-data@2.3.3
node_modules/request/node_modules/form-data
  form-data@"~2.3.2" from request@2.88.2
  node_modules/request
    request@"2.88.2" from node-ses@3.0.3
    node_modules/node-ses
      node-ses@"^3.0.3" from @strapi/provider-email-amazon-ses@5.23.4
      packages/providers/email-amazon-ses
        @strapi/provider-email-amazon-ses@5.23.4
        node_modules/@strapi/provider-email-amazon-ses
          workspace packages/providers/email-amazon-ses from the root project
```

A quick investigation shows that that package does not support multipart messages, and always encodes query parameters when using `request`, thus `form-data` is never used, so it is highly unlikely there is a vulnerability present. Regardless, replacing node-ses with a better maintained alternative is on our roadmap.

### Why is it needed?

GHSA-95m3-7q98-8xr5
GHSA-fjxv-7rqg-78g4
GHSA-4hjh-wcwx-xvwj

### How to test it?

Everything should work as before

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Let us know if this is related to any issue/pull request

title: enhancement: add unrelated data to dummy preview
body:
### What does it do?

Updates the dummy-preview page (the page we use as the preview within getstarted) so that it also fetches and displays content from another entry. This simulates better real websites that usually fetch content from multiple sources in Strapi at once.

⚠️ this PR only affects the testing environment we created for Preview in examples/getstarted. No need to do pre-release QA for it.

### Why is it needed?

Having this in getstarted is useful because:
- we display a "This field comes from a different document" toast when the user double clicks on a highlight that's from a different document. Now we can test this behavior.
- at some point we'll actually support editing things from different documents (see this [internal poc](https://strapihq.slack.com/archives/C092SNX8FB6/p1757922405127179)). At that point we'll be happy to have the environment ready to test it

### How to test it?

- Open preview for any entry
- Scroll to the bottom of the preview, see the "unrelated api data" section
- Double click on the title field to edit it: you should see a toast saying "This field comes from a different document"

title: enhancement(session-manager): allow httpOnly option and remove familyType
body:
### What does it do?


- allow optionally setting httpOnly to avoid having that value returned and only use httpOnly cookie instead
  - this can be forced globally with up config `sessions.httpOnly:true` or if that is not set, on a per-reqeust basis setting the  header `x-strapi-refresh-cookie` = 'httpOnly'
- return refresh token when rotating it without httpOnly
- use 'type' instead of 'familyType'

### Why is it needed?

previously the refreshToken was not actually being sent again which was a bug
this way we send it, but also have a way for it to be more secure using httpOnly

title: Fix: content api route serialization
body:
<!--
Hello 👋 Thank you for submitting a pull request.

To help us merge your PR, make sure to follow the instructions below:

- Create or update the tests
- Create or update the documentation at https://github.com/strapi/documentation
- Refer to the issue you are closing in the PR description: Fix #issue
- Specify if the PR is ready to be merged or work in progress (by opening a draft PR)

Please ensure you read the Contributing Guide: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

### What does it do?

- Adds route serialization utilities to ensure content API route objects are safe to JSON-serialize:
  - `packages/core/utils/src/route-serialization.ts`
  - Exported via `packages/core/utils/src/index.ts`
- Applies sanitization where content API routes are exposed to the admin:
  - `packages/core/core/src/services/content-api/index.ts` → `getRoutesMap()` now returns a sanitized routes map
  - `packages/plugins/users-permissions/server/services/users-permissions.js` → `getRoutes()` now returns a sanitized routes map

The sanitization strips `request` and `response` (Zod) properties from route objects to avoid circular/non-serializable data.

### Why is it needed?

Some content API routes are returned to the admin panel (e.g. API Tokens permissions UI, Users & Permissions). Route objects can carry Zod schemas in `request` and `response` which are not JSON-serializable and can cause runtime serialization errors when building the admin UI. Sanitizing these fields ensures the responses sent to the admin are plain JSON.

### How to test it?

- Verify admin endpoints return serializable JSON:
  1. Core admin routes map
     - Perform: `GET /admin/content-api/routes`
     - Expect: 200 with JSON map of routes.
  2. Users & Permissions routes
     - Perform: `GET /users-permissions/permissions/routes`
     - Expect: 200 with JSON map of routes; similarly sanitized.
- UI validation (optional):
  - In Users & Permissions → Roles, ensure permissions/routing data loads correctly.

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

Resolves https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/21478
author: jhoward1994

reviews.body: QAed locally, everything seems to work well, LGTM!
LGTM!
Good for me, saw with @innerdvations that we will see to drop lodash later since this one needs to be merged today!

title: App integration of session manager authentication
body:
### What does it do?

- **Adds session-based admin authentication** 
  - Issues refresh cookie `strapi_admin_refresh` on login/register; exchanges it for short‑lived access tokens via `POST /admin/access-token`.
  - Admin strategy validates Bearer access tokens via the session manager and requires an active session;
  - `POST /admin/logout` clears the cookie and revokes refresh tokens for the user; optionally scoped by `deviceId`.
- **Extends admin auth contracts and validation**:
- **Admin UI integration**:
  - Adds `getOrCreateDeviceId()` (localStorage UUID) and passes `deviceId`/`rememberMe` on login/register; logout includes `deviceId`.
- **Tests**: Adds API tests for access-token exchange, logout revocation (device-scoped and global), renew aliasing, and strategy behavior; adds unit tests for the session manager.

### Why is it needed?

- Improves admin auth security and UX with short‑lived access tokens and revocable, device‑scoped refresh sessions.
- Enables cookie‑based flows by default
- Allows targeted or global l§ogout and proper session invalidation.

### How to test it?

- Login via `POST /admin/login` with `{ email, password, rememberMe?, deviceId? }`:
  - Verify `Set-Cookie: strapi_admin_refresh=...` (Expires present if `rememberMe` is true).
  - Response `data` contains `token` , and `refreshToken` for non-cookie clients.
- Exchange refresh for access: `POST /admin/access-token` with the cookie, or `{ refreshToken }` in body; expect `200` and `data.token`.
- Call a protected route (e.g. `GET /admin/users/me`) with `Authorization: Bearer <access token>`; expect `200`.
- Logout: `POST /admin/logout` (optionally `{ deviceId }`); cookie is expired and sessions are revoked accordingly.
- `POST /admin/renew-token` returns deprecation headers and behaves like `/admin/access-token`
- Run the added API tests in `tests/api/core/admin/*sessions*.test.api.ts` and the session manager unit tests.

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)
author: jhoward1994

reviews.body: (tested and reviewed live) LGTM!

title: future(preview): better highlight click handling and errors
body:
### What does it do?

Another PR with unrelated changes, sorry about that.

- fixes the single/double click to edit behavior on preview highlights.
  - single clicks: it dispatches the click on the underlying element. So if a button has Strapi content, clicking it should click the button. I made the "email" field a button clickable in dummy-preview so we can test that. It also now sends a notification so users know they should double click to edit
  - double click: should open the input popover. Even when double clicking on a button. That was broken before.
- refactors previewScript a bit:
  - now it's the highlights that receive the click events, and that re-dispatch the click to the underlying element if needed. That's in order to make the single/double clicks work, the previous approach seemed like a dead end.
  - does as much as possible in CSS instead of in JS. So no more mouseenter and mouseleave handlers, just hover styles. It should be better for performance
- Adds translated notification messages so users are aware of the known limitations

### How to test it?

- Single and double a bunch of stuff, in particular the email button
- Try to edit a field from a relation

title: future(preview): handle nested fields
body:
### What does it do?

Adds handling of nested fields to the new Preview. To summarize the changes needed:
- we now encode a lot more data into the stega source maps. We add the model, documentId, kind, locale and field path. They're all the information we need to find exactly where a field comes from in Strapi.
- in order to get the field path, I had to add a new `rawWithIndices` to the `traverseEntity` util. It's the same as the already existing raw property, but it also has information about the index in an array. For example, for a field that's in the 2nd component in a dynamic zone, raw is `dz.field`, rawWithIndices is `dz.1.field`. I'm not modifying `raw` directly because I think it would be a breaking change.
- we now have 2 separate listeners for events coming from the preview:
  - in `pages/Preview.tsx`: the one about the initial feature configuration
  - in `components/InputPopover`: the one about events happening on a specific field. When it receives an event, we already have all the required data (schema, document, etc.) ready to handle it
- added utils to retrieve the schema of a field based on the field path

### Why is it needed?

Can't edit much without nested fields, components and DZs are everywhere

### How to test it?

Open preview for the kitchensink content type. Try the components, repeatable components, and dynamic zone.
author: remidej

reviews.body: The code looks good to me, we discussed it together yesterday too so I don't have many comments. The addition of the indexes in the traverse util was a bit more involved than I thought it would be. Still really surprised we didn't already have that info available 🤔. In the side editor if I'm editing a field and I hit enter, it saves (I thought it was cmd + enter? but anyway...), in the popover should we expect the same behavior? If I hit enter currently in the popover nothing happens. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/863ae3bf-ac2b-4421-9b61-be15bb44be29 I cannot edit a null value in the preview using a popover. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0e606b95-9d89-4fb1-86cc-55206b2e4c0c We do not see nested components in the preview. Example below is in DZ but same issue with a component at the root level. IMO a two-way scroll into view would improve the UX. I click a field in the side editor, scroll into view the field in the preview and show the highlight. I click the field in the preview, scroll into view in the side editor. Maybe technically that is challenging with the user preview. I can't remember if we already discussed this 🤦‍♂️ .
LGTM!

title: future(preview): add content source maps service
body: ### What does it do? - Creates a content-source-maps service. It traverses entries to attach field sourcemaps as stega encoded data when possible in the content API. This only runs when the `encode-source-maps` header is passed. - Adds the logic in our previewScript to detect fields with encoded data, and write that data to the data-attributes so we can show our highlight overlays for them Known issues: - not all fields are handled, only the string-based ones. - the actual data we encode is quite dumb, improving that will be the next PR I used 2 references for inspiration: - Alex's [v4 sourcemaps plugin](https://github.com/strapi/plugin-content-source-map/blob/main/server/src/encoder.ts) (RIP) for the actual encoding logic - the v4 `strapi-response-format` functionality to see how to transform the content API responses based on a header ### Why is it needed? This metadata powers our new Live Preview ### How to test it? The most convenient way is to open the preview page for any entry on getstarted, and scroll down in the preview to the code block. This should let you see the weird invisible characters:

title: feat(data-transfer): add directory export/import format
body:
### What does it do?

- Adds **directory-based Strapi Data Transfer (DTS) export/import**: the CLI accepts an **unpacked export directory** (same layout as inside a `.tar`: `metadata.json`, `schemas/`, `entities/`, `links/`, `configuration/`, `assets/`, …) in addition to `.tar` / `.tar.gz` / `.tar.enc` archives.
- Introduces **directory source (and related) providers** in `@strapi/data-transfer`, wired through the public package surface and Strapi CLI import/export flow (including validation that a path looks like an unpacked export).
- Refactors the Strapi core CLI to load Inquirer via a small **`getInquirer()`** helper (cached dynamic import) instead of repeating `await import('inquirer')` at many call sites.
- Fixes **`@strapi/cloud-cli`**: `deploy-project/action.ts` no longer references a removed `loadInquirer` symbol (uses the same dynamic import pattern as elsewhere in that package).
- Moves **e2e DTS fixtures** under `tests/e2e/data/` from committed `.tar` blobs to **unpacked directories** so JSON/JSONL and small metadata files are normal text diffs in Git; binary uploads stay under `assets/uploads/` as before.
- Updates **contributor / e2e documentation** for the new fixture layout and import workflow.
- Adjusts **tooling** so fixture trees are not churned by repo-wide formatting: `.prettierignore` entries for `tests/e2e/data/**` and `**/tmp-dts-export/**` (e.g. `examples/complex/tmp-dts-export`), plus optional **EditorConfig** overrides for those paths so editor save behavior doesn’t fight fixture files.

### Why is it needed?

- **Unpacked directories** make export/import fixtures easier to **review, diff, and update** in Git than opaque archives, while keeping the same on-disk layout Strapi already uses inside a transfer archive.
- **Single, cached Inquirer load** in the core CLI avoids duplicated dynamic imports and keeps ESM/CJS interop predictable across CLI commands.
- The **cloud CLI** build broke after removing `loadInquirer`; this restores a valid implementation without pulling core helpers into cloud-cli.
- **Prettier / format-on-save** was still touching `metadata.json` and similar files under example export trees; ignoring those paths prevents noisy EOF/newline-only diffs when running `yarn prettier:write`.

### How to test it?

**Environment:** Node/Yarn as in the repo root; build Strapi packages if your workflow requires it (`yarn setup` / `yarn build` as you normally use for local dev).

**Automated**

- Unit tests: `@strapi/data-transfer` directory provider tests and Strapi CLI import/export/transfer/admin command tests that were updated on this branch.
- CLI tests: `tests/cli/tests/strapi/data-transfer/` (export, import, roundtrip) — they exercise export → `output.tar` → import using the test app and e2e-backed seed data.
- E2e: run the Playwright suite or the subset that resets DB from `tests/e2e/data` (e.g. `with-admin`) if you touch import paths or fixtures.

**Manual**

- From a Strapi app, run **`strapi export`** to a filename (producing a `.tar` as today), then extract it and run **`strapi import -f /path/to/unpacked/dir`** (or point `-f` at the directory directly, per CLI help) and confirm import completes.
- Optionally compare behavior importing the **same content** as `.tar` vs unpacked directory (DB / admin content parity).
- Run **`yarn prettier:write`** and confirm **`tests/e2e/data/**`** and **`examples/complex/tmp-dts-export/**`** files are not rewritten (no spurious newline-only changes to `metadata.json`).

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

CMS-538

title: fix(content-manager): wrap single type displayName with formatMessage
body:
## Description

The edit view heading for single types renders `schema.info.displayName` as a raw string without passing it through `formatMessage`. This means custom admin translations registered via `config.translations` in `app.js` are ignored for single type page headings, while they work correctly for:

- The sidebar/left menu (uses `formatMessage` in `LeftMenu.tsx`)
- The collection type list view heading (uses `formatMessage`)

This PR wraps the single type displayName with `formatMessage`, matching the pattern used in LeftMenu:

```js
// Before
return schema.info.displayName;

// After
return formatMessage({
  id: schema.info.displayName,
  defaultMessage: schema.info.displayName,
});
```

This tries to find a translation for the displayName first, falling back to the raw string if none exists — exactly how `LeftMenu.tsx:70` handles link titles.

## How to reproduce

1. Create a single type with `displayName: "About"`
2. Add a translation override in `src/admin/app.js`
3. Navigate to the single type edit view — heading shows raw "About" instead of the translation

## Related Issue

Fixes #25829
author: guoyangzhen

reviews.body:
## Claude Code Review

This pull request is from a fork — automated review is disabled. A repository maintainer can comment `@claude review` to run a one-time review.

commits.message:
fix(content-manager): wrap single type displayName with formatMessage

The edit view heading for single types was rendering schema.info.displayName
as a raw string without passing it through formatMessage. This meant custom
admin translations registered via config.translations were ignored.

Now uses formatMessage({ id: displayName, defaultMessage: displayName })
to match how LeftMenu handles link titles — tries translation first,
falls back to raw displayName.

Fixes #25829

title: fix: change return to continue in deleteRelations when using foreign keys
body:
Fixes #25855

## Problem

In `packages/core/database/src/entity-manager/index.ts`, the `deleteRelations` method uses `return` instead of `continue` when `db.dialect.usesForeignKeys()` is true. This causes the function to exit the entire handler early, skipping all remaining relation attributes.

## Root Cause

```ts
if (db.dialect.usesForeignKeys()) {
  return;  // exits entire function, not just current iteration
}
```

The code is inside a loop over attributes. When any attribute hits this condition, `return` exits the entire `deleteRelations` function instead of just skipping to the next attribute.

## Fix

Changed `return` to `continue` to properly skip the current attribute and continue processing remaining attributes.
author: guoyangzhen

reviews.body:
## Claude Code Review

This pull request is from a fork — automated review is disabled. A repository maintainer can comment `@claude review` to run a one-time review.

fix: change return to continue in deleteRelations when using foreign keys

The deleteRelations method used 'return' instead of 'continue' when
db.dialect.usesForeignKeys() was true, causing the function to exit
early and skip processing all remaining relation attributes.

Fixes #25855

title: fix(create-strapi-app): generate .yarnrc.yml for Yarn projects
body:
## What does it do?

Updates `create-strapi-app` so newly scaffolded projects using Yarn automatically include a `.yarnrc.yml` file with:

```yml
nodeLinker: node-modules
```

The file is only created when Yarn is the selected package manager, and scaffold coverage was added to verify:
- Yarn projects get `.yarnrc.yml`
- npm projects do not get `.yarnrc.yml`

## Why is it needed?

New Strapi apps created with Yarn 3+ can fail to run `develop` or `build` because Yarn Plug'n'Play is enabled by default and `esbuild-register` ends up requiring `esbuild` in a way that is incompatible with that setup.

Generating `.yarnrc.yml` with `nodeLinker: node-modules` avoids the PnP resolution issue and makes freshly created Yarn-based Strapi apps runnable out of the box.

## How to test it?

Environment:
- Yarn `3+`

Verification path:

1. Create a new app with Yarn:

```bash
node packages/cli/create-strapi-app/bin/index.js strapi-yarn-app --non-interactive --skip-cloud --no-git-init --no-install --use-yarn
```

2. Verify the generated file exists:

Expected result:

```yml
nodeLinker: node-modules
```

3. Create a new app with npm:

```bash
node packages/cli/create-strapi-app/bin/index.js strapi-npm-app --non-interactive --skip-cloud --no-git-init --no-install --use-npm
```

4. Verify `.yarnrc.yml` is not created for npm:

can be tested with the experimental as well: `0.0.0-experimental.90669470e7ec88f45eeadc88ae2b17fc3b556547`

## Related issue(s)/PR(s)

fixes #25775

This is a security release fixing several security relevant issues.

Changelog

Release history

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

  • Changelogs are for humans, not machines.
  • There should be an entry for every single version.
  • The same types of changes should be grouped.
  • Versions and sections should be linkable.
  • The latest version comes first.
  • The release date of each versions is displayed.
  • Mention whether you follow Semantic Versioning.

Changelog entries are classified using the following labels (from keep-a-changelog):

  • Added for new features.
  • Changed for changes in existing functionality.
  • Deprecated for soon-to-be removed features.
  • Removed for now removed features.
  • Fixed for any bug fixes.
  • Security in case of vulnerabilities.

4.0.0 (2024-02-07)

Fixes

  • Fix bad text values in parse #126, thanks to @​connor4312

Changed

  • Remove process global to work outside of node #129, thanks to @​styfle
  • Add sideEffects to package.json #128, thanks to @​frandiox
  • Removed os, make compatible browser environment. See #124, thanks to @​gwsbhqt

title: chore(deps): bump path-to-regexp from 8.4.0 to 8.4.1
body:
Bumps [path-to-regexp](https://github.com/pillarjs/path-to-regexp) from 8.4.0 to 8.4.1.
Release notes

Sourced from path-to-regexp's releases.

8.4.1

Fixed

  • Remove trie deduplication (#431) 6bc8e84
    • Using a trie required non-greedy matching, which regressed wildcards in non-ending mode by matching them up until the first match. For example:
      • /*foo with /a/b = /a
      • /*foo.htmlwith /a/b.html/c.html = /a/b.html
  • Allow backtrack handling to match itself (#427) 5bcd30b
    • When backtracking was introduced, it rejected matching things like /:"a"_:"b" against /foo__. This makes intuitive sense because the second parameter is not going to backtrack on anymore, but it's somewhat unexpected since there's no reason it shouldn't match the second`.

https://github.com/pillarjs/path-to-regexp/compare/v8.4.0...v8.4.1

title: chore(deps): bump js-yaml from 3.14.1 to 3.14.2
body:
Bumps [js-yaml](https://github.com/nodeca/js-yaml) from 3.14.1 to 3.14.2.
Changelog

Sourced from js-yaml's changelog.

[3.14.2] - 2025-11-15

Security

  • Backported v4.1.1 fix to v3

[4.1.1] - 2025-11-12

Security

  • Fix prototype pollution issue in yaml merge (<<) operator.

[4.1.0] - 2021-04-15

Added

  • Types are now exported as yaml.types.XXX.
  • Every type now has options property with original arguments kept as they were (see yaml.types.int.options as an example).

Changed

  • Schema.extend() now keeps old type order in case of conflicts (e.g. Schema.extend([ a, b, c ]).extend([ b, a, d ]) is now ordered as abcd instead of cbad).

[4.0.0] - 2021-01-03

Changed

  • Check migration guide to see details for all breaking changes.
  • Breaking: "unsafe" tags !!js/function, !!js/regexp, !!js/undefined are moved to js-yaml-js-types package.
  • Breaking: removed safe* functions. Use load, loadAll, dump instead which are all now safe by default.
  • yaml.DEFAULT_SAFE_SCHEMA and yaml.DEFAULT_FULL_SCHEMA are removed, use yaml.DEFAULT_SCHEMA instead.
  • yaml.Schema.create(schema, tags) is removed, use schema.extend(tags) instead.
  • !!binary now always mapped to Uint8Array on load.
  • Reduced nesting of /lib folder.
  • Parse numbers according to YAML 1.2 instead of YAML 1.1 (01234 is now decimal, 0o1234 is octal, 1:23 is parsed as string instead of base60).
  • dump() no longer quotes :, [, ], (, ) except when necessary, #470, #557.
  • Line and column in exceptions are now formatted as (X:Y) instead of at line X, column Y (also present in compact format), #332.
  • Code snippet created in exceptions now contains multiple lines with line numbers.
  • dump() now serializes undefined as null in collections and removes keys with undefined in mappings, #571.
  • dump() with skipInvalid=true now serializes invalid items in collections as null.
  • Custom tags starting with ! are now dumped as !tag instead of !<!tag>, #576.
  • Custom tags starting with tag:yaml.org,2002: are now shorthanded using !!, #258.

Added

  • Added .mjs (es modules) support.
  • Added quotingType and forceQuotes options for dumper to configure string literal style, #290, #529.
  • Added styles: { '!!null': 'empty' } option for dumper (serializes { foo: null } as "foo: "), #570.

... (truncated)

title: RBAC "Move to stage" for review workflows
body:
### What does it do?

- **Adds "to" stage permissions for Review Workflows.** Previously, permissions only controlled who can move content *out* of a stage ("from"). This change adds explicit "to" permissions so you can control which roles can move content *into* each stage.
- **Transition rules:** Moving from stage A → B now requires: (1) "From" permission on stage A (unchanged), and (2) "To" permission on stage B (new).
- **Backward compatibility:** If a stage has no "to" permissions configured, all roles are allowed to move into it (same as before).

### Why is it needed?

Today, any role with "from" permission on a stage can move content to *any* other stage. That makes it impossible to restrict who can move content into sensitive stages (e.g. "Published"), so Editors could bypass review and publish directly. "To" permissions close that gap and allow proper RBAC for review workflows (e.g. only Admins can move into "Published").

### How to test it?

1. **Unit/API tests:** Run `yarn jest packages/core/review-workflows` and the new API test `tests/api/core/review-workflows/review-workflows-stage-permissions.test.api.ts` (or your project's API test command for that path).
2. **Manual:** Create a workflow (e.g. Draft → Review → Published). Set "to" permission for "Published" to Admin only; leave "from" as usual (e.g. Editor + Admin). 
As Editor: confirm you can move Draft → Review, but "Published" is not in the stage dropdown and a direct API call to move to Published returns 403. 
As Admin: confirm you can move to Published. 
Create or use a workflow with no "to" permissions; confirm all roles can still move into those stages (backward compatibility).

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

- Fixes CMS-314
author: jhoward1994

reviews.body: Tested it and looks like it's working, nice work
I QAed and it works well, nice work! if we can take this as an opportunity to improve some types it'd make some parts a lot easier :P

Great work Jamie! 
I suggested changing the stageObject's field name from `permissions` to `fromPermissions` to better match the contract and UI part, it's not blocking, but if you do decide to change the field name, don't forget to update the API tests as they now will be expecting `fromPermissions` as request body 😄
LGTM, great job :D

commits.message: test: add api tests for to stage permissions
feat: migration for backward compatible stage transfer permissions
chore: rename permissions to fromPermissions in workflow stages

body:

Release notes

Sourced from @​xmldom/xmldom's releases.

0.8.12

Commits

Fixed

  • preserve trailing whitespace in ProcessingInstruction data [#962](https://github.com/xmldom/xmldom/issues/962) / [#42](https://github.com/xmldom/xmldom/issues/42)
  • Security: createCDATASection now throws InvalidCharacterError when data contains "]]>", as required by the WHATWG DOM spec. GHSA-wh4c-j3r5-mjhp
  • Security: XMLSerializer now splits CDATASection nodes whose data contains "]]>" into adjacent CDATA sections at serialization time, preventing XML injection via mutation methods (appendData, replaceData, .data =, .textContent =). GHSA-wh4c-j3r5-mjhp

Code that passes a string containing "]]>" to createCDATASection and relied on the previously unsafe behavior will now receive InvalidCharacterError. Use a mutation method such as appendData if you intentionally need "]]>" in a CDATASection node's data.

Thank you, @​thesmartshadow, @​stevenobiajulu, for your contributions

title: fix(content-manager): pass component schemas when rebuilding list view headers
body:
### What does it do?

- Exposes **`listViewConversionContext`** from `useDocumentLayout`: component configuration from the content-type config, component schemas from `useDocument`, and all content-type schemas (same data `formatListLayout` already passes into `convertListLayoutToFieldLayouts`).
- Updates **`ListViewPage`** so when it rebuilds **persisted list column names** into field layouts, it calls `convertListLayoutToFieldLayouts` with that full context—not only `(columns, attributes, metadatas)`.
- Adds **`convertListLayoutToFieldLayouts` unit tests** and extends **`useDocumentLayout` tests** to cover the new hook field.

### Why is it needed?

Renaming a **component field label** in Content Manager → Configure the view (label only) could break the **list view** with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'attributes')` in `getMainField`. That path re-ran `convertListLayoutToFieldLayouts` without component schemas, so `getMainField` could not read the nested component attribute used for `mainField`. The server layout path already passed the right arguments; the persisted-header path did not.

### How to test it?

1. Create a collection type with a **component** field; add fields inside the component (e.g. `name`).
2. Create at least one entry so the component has data.
3. Open **Configure the view** for that collection type and **change only the component field’s label** (e.g. `user` → `User`), save.
4. Go back to the **list view** for that collection type.

**Expected:** List loads without error; component column still shows correctly.

**Automated:** From the repo root, build the content-manager package if your workflow requires it, then run the content-manager front tests (e.g. Jest for `convertListLayoutToFieldLayouts.test.ts` and `useDocumentLayout.test.ts`).

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

- https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/25509

title: fix(documentation): use dist extensions path in production
body:
## Summary

Fixes #22701

The documentation plugin hardcodes `strapi.dirs.app.extensions` (`src/extensions/`) for reading OpenAPI spec files. In production deployments, these files live under `dist/src/extensions/` after the build step. Since `src/` is typically not deployed to production servers, accessing the documentation page results in:

```
Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, scandir './src/extensions/documentation/documentation'
```

This was introduced by #21950 and has been confirmed on versions 5.8.0 through 5.30.0.

## Root Cause

The documentation plugin uses `strapi.dirs.app.extensions` (which resolves to `src/extensions/`) for both reading and writing documentation files. In development this works because `src/` exists. In production, files are compiled to `dist/src/extensions/` but the plugin still reads from `src/`.

Other Strapi core loaders (plugins, components, APIs, middlewares) correctly use `strapi.dirs.dist.*` — the documentation plugin was the outlier.

## Changes

**`packages/plugins/documentation/server/src/services/documentation.ts`**
- `getFullDocumentationPath()`: use `strapi.dirs.dist.extensions` in production
- `getApiDocumentationPath()`: use `strapi.dirs.dist.extensions` / `strapi.dirs.dist.api` in production

**`packages/plugins/documentation/server/src/controllers/documentation.ts`**
- `index()` route: use production-aware path for reading specs and serving HTML

In development, the original `app.extensions` path is preserved for writing generated docs.

## Test plan

- [ ] Build Strapi for production (`strapi build`)
- [ ] Remove the `src/` directory
- [ ] Start in production mode (`NODE_ENV=production strapi start`)
- [ ] Access the documentation page — should load without ENOENT errors
- [ ] In development mode, documentation generation still works as before
author: R41D1

commits.message:
fix(documentation): use dist extensions path in production

The documentation plugin hardcoded strapi.dirs.app.extensions (src/)
for reading OpenAPI spec files. In production, these files live under
dist/src/extensions/ after the build step, causing ENOENT errors when
the src/ directory is not deployed.

Use strapi.dirs.dist.extensions in production for both the service
(getFullDocumentationPath, getApiDocumentationPath) and the controller
(index route serving the spec and HTML). In development, the original
app.extensions path is preserved for writing generated docs.

Fixes #22701

title: fix(core): relation handling preserves order during unpublish/republish cycles
body:
## What does it do?

When a content entry is unpublished and then republished, the `load()` function in `bidirectional-relations.ts` now also retrieves the relation order from the draft link table

It maps the draft-side join-table rows to their corresponding future published rows, so that `sync()` can restore the correct relation order on the newly created published link rows.

## Why is this needed?

When an entry involved in a bidirectional many-to-many relation is unpublished and later republished, its published join-table rows are deleted on unpublish and then recreated on publish.

## How to test

1. Create a content type with a many-to-many bidirectional relation (e.g. Article ↔ Author), ensuring both types have Draft & Publish enabled.
2. Create several related entries (e.g., Article with Authors A, B, C in that order) and publish all of them.
3. Verify that the published Article's author order is [A, B, C].
4. Unpublish Author B, then republish Author B.
5. Check that the published Article still lists authors in the order [A, B, C].

An API integration test that covers this scenario can be found at:
`tests/api/core/strapi/document-service/relations/bidirectional-relations.test.api.ts`

## Related issues/PRs

- CMS-166
author: jhoward1994

reviews.body:
## Claude Code Review

This repository is configured for manual code reviews. Comment `@claude review` to trigger a review.

This does seem to fix the primary case, and doesn't appear to break any existing paths, but it still misses owner-side sync and drops rows when related entries aren't published yet. I sent you some more detailed info on Slack in case you want to address that here!
LGTM!

commits.message: fix(core): relation handling preserves order during unpublish/republish cycles

fix: preserve bidirectional order (#25792)

- Keep join rows when related side is still draft; map to published id when available
- Sync owner-side join order when publishing the owning type (inversedBy path)
- Refactor bidirectional load into shared capture + draft→published mapping helpers

title: fix: contains filter no longer returns empty data
body:
### What does it do?

traverse scalar fields when the value is an operator object so filter handling matches nested structures used by the REST API

### Why is it needed?

Fixes REST filtering with **`$contains` / `$containsi`** on scalar fields sometimes returning empty datasets when matches should exist ([#25795](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/25795)) because generated queries were incorrect

yarn build
yarn test:unit packages/core/utils/src/__tests__/sanitize.visitors.test.ts
yarn test:unit packages/core/utils/src/__tests__/validate.visitors.test.ts
yarn test:api tests/api/core/strapi/filtering.test.api.js
